title: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot
preview:
From: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Cc: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medtech.com>
Date: Mon, 28 Jun 2026 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Hi Alex / Priya,

Thanks again for last week's demo. As we discussed, the steering committee needs a short mutual action plan so we can lock a pilot decision by mid-July. Two things are blocking the contract: (1) clear hosting recommendation (Hosted API vs Dedicated capacity vs Private deployment) for our pilot dataset (PHI subset, US-only), and (2) a validated timeline with security gating (SOC 2 evidence + our questionnaire).

Attached is our draft MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx) and our security questionnaire (security_questionnaire.pdf). High level asks from our side:
- Deployment mode recommendation with tradeoffs (latency, cost, data residency, SLA)
- Estimated run costs for a 3-month pilot (token volumes attached in MAP)
- Evaluation gates and minimum success criteria (latency P95, throughput, false-positive rate on redaction)
- Region options — we prefer us-east for latency but need clarification on data residency and encryption in transit/at rest.

Our steering committee wants a firm commit or a clear go/no-go path by 2026-07-15 so procurement can start PO paperwork. Can you send a proposed MAP with milestones + dates and a recommended hosting mode by EOD 2026-06-30?

Thanks —
Brenda
Brenda Long
Head of Platform Partnerships, MedTech Inc.
brenda.long@medtech.com
+1 (415) 555-0129

Attachments: MedTech_MAP_v2.xlsx (spreadsheet), security_questionnaire.pdf (PDF, 12 pages)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Brenda — thanks for the clear ask and the attachments. Drafting a concise MAP and recommendation now. High-level response below; I will attach an updated MAP workbook (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) by EOD Tuesday.

Short recommendation (pilot scope):
- Recommended mode: Hosted API (us-east-1) for fastest time-to-value and to avoid initial procurement friction.
  - Pros: no infra procurement, Redwood manages SLAs and autoscaling, fastest onboarding (1–2 weeks).
  - Cons: shared tenancy (we provide strict data isolation controls), possibly higher unit costs vs Dedicated.
- Dedicated capacity: attractive if you need predictable SLOs + cost controls for sustained high QPS; requires reserved capacity contract and ~4–6 week lead time.
- Private (VPC/on‑prem): recommended only if legal requires customer-controlled keys or air-gap. Longer setup (6–10+ weeks) and heavier Ops.

Security gating: we can provide SOC 2 artifacts and a completed questionnaire. We will also propose an encrypted-in-transit and encrypted-at-rest default for the pilot, and offer customer-managed KMS if you need key separation (this pulls the thread toward Dedicated/Private).

Proposed MAP milestones (short):
1) Security review complete (Redwood provides SOC 2 + questionnaire answers) — target: 2026-07-06
2) Technical eval (integration + smoke tests, 7 days) — target window: 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13
3) Performance gate (latency P95 < 150ms for typical prompt mix OR documented fallback to smaller model) — by 2026-07-13
4) Contract & PO signoff — target: 2026-07-15

To hit these dates we need:
- Rishi / infra team: quick costed estimate for Dedicated reserved pool (3-month pilot profile) and delta vs hosted (units).
- CS (Priya): success criteria & canary plan for integration and data loading.
- MedTech: confirmation on whether customer-managed KMS or standard Redwood keys are acceptable for the pilot.

Looping in Rishi for the cost numbers. Priya — can you draft the canary plan? I will prepare the updated MAP and call out the decisions that shift us between Hosted/Dedicated/Private.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0190

From: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Brenda Long <brenda.long@medtech.com>
Date: Tue, 29 Jun 2026 13:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Evaluation gates & MAP — hosting decision for clinical pilot

Thanks Alex — happy to own the evaluation plan from CS side. Quick notes on canary & success criteria:

Canary plan (7 days):
- Day 0–1: integration (API keys, test dataset upload — limited to synthetic/hashed PHI)
- Day 2–3: baseline latency and throughput tests (our harness + Redwood streaming)
- Day 4–5: end-to-end functional checks with redaction metrics and downstream consumer validation
- Day 6–7: stakeholder review and go/no-go decision

Success criteria (minimum):
- Latency: P95 < 200ms for typical prompt mix (prefer <150ms)
- Availability: >= 99% during canary window
- Redaction/PII handling: automated redaction passes 95% of vetted prompts
- Cost threshold: pilot projected cost + 20% buffer <= $12k/month (per MAP numbers)

If Hosted path is chosen we can be live faster; for Dedicated we should start PO conversations immediately (Rishi — can you provide a ballpark reserved capacity estimate for 3 months by 2026-07-01?).

Thanks —
Priya Natarajan
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
priya.natarajan@redwood.com

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>
Cc: Priya Natarajan <priya.natarajan@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 30 Jun 2026 08:10:00 -0700
Subject: Internal: quick estimate for MedTech pilot (dedicated vs hosted)

Rishi / Marcus —

Following MedTech's MAP request (see thread), we need a short internal runbook and two cost points by EOD 2026-07-01:
- Hosted API estimate (3-month pilot): monthly token profile is in the MAP. Provide expected monthly spend and expected p95 latency in us-east-1.
- Dedicated reservation estimate (3-month pilot): upfront reservation delta, monthly burn, and time-to-provision (weeks). Include any special KMS/HSM integration overhead if customer-managed keys are requested.

Constraints: customer will accept Hosted if we can supply SOC 2 + questionnaire and meet latency P95 <150–200ms. If keys are customer-managed, they prefer Dedicated or Private.

Please paste short bullets here; I will fold into the MAP and send to Brenda.

Thanks,
Alex

Brenda — attaching the updated MAP (MedTech_MAP_v2_redwood_comments.xlsx) and the Redwood security questionnaire responses. Quick summary and ask:

Executive summary / recommended path:
- Recommendation for pilot: Hosted API in us-east-1 to meet your mid-July timeline and minimize procurement friction. We will provide SOC 2 artifacts and completed questionnaire.
- If MedTech requires customer-managed KMS or hardware isolation, the alternative is Dedicated capacity in a single-tenant pool (provisioning time: ~3–5 weeks) or Private deployment (6–10+ weeks).

Updated MAP milestones & dates:
- 2026-07-01: Redwood provides completed security questionnaire + SOC 2 artifacts (DONE — attached)
- 2026-07-06: Security review complete (MedTech confirms)
- 2026-07-07 to 2026-07-13: Technical canary (see Priya's plan)
- 2026-07-13: Performance gate & stakeholder review
- 2026-07-15: Contract & PO signoff (target)

Action items for MedTech:
- Confirm whether customer-managed KMS is required for pilot (reply to this thread by 2026-07-01).
- Approve the MAP milestones above or propose adjust by 2026-07-01 so we can align procurement.

Action items for Redwood:
- Rishi: dedicated vs hosted cost delta (see internal note) — deliver by 2026-07-01.
- Priya: run canary plan and engineering handoff — ready to start on 2026-07-07.

If those dates look good I will schedule a 30-minute alignment call on 2026-07-02 to walk the steering committee through the MAP and the hosting tradeoffs. Please confirm availability.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

title: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 09:17:00 -07:00
Subject: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the run-through yesterday. To make the dedicated capacity plan concrete for your SRE and procurement teams, I attached two artifacts: a capacity-modeling workbook (edgehealth_capacity_modeling.xlsx) and a proposed ops-ownership matrix (ops-ownership-matrix.csv).

As discussed, we want to align on:
- Peak concurrency and expected burst patterns (typical business hours vs promo windows)
- Target latency SLOs (p50/p90/p99 tail targets and acceptable degradation under burst)
- Region preference for primary capacity and acceptable failover region(s)
- Preferred burst mechanism: reserved capacity + controlled burst vs ephemeral multi-region spillover
- Who owns day-to-day pool lifecycle (EdgeHealth SRE vs Redwood CS/ops) and on-call escalation path

Quick asks for your team so we can finalize the provisioning plan and commit timelines:
1) Confirm a 30d forecast for concurrent requests and a list of 3 expected high-traffic windows (dates/times).
2) Provide p90 sequence length estimate (tokens) and percent of requests using streaming vs non-streaming generation.
3) Confirm preferred primary region (us-west1 / us-east1 / europe-west1) and whether cross-region failover is allowed.
4) Introduce procurement contact (procure@edgehealth.ai is fine) so Marissa can start lead-time discussions.

We’ll run two provisioning scenarios in the workbook:
- Scenario A (Reserved + Burst): baseline reserved pool sized for steady-state + a defined burst band that pre-warms nodes and uses queued admission for short spikes.
- Scenario B (Multi-region spill): smaller baseline but with automated spill to secondary region when latency breach is imminent.

If you can reply with the numbers above by EOD Friday (July 2), Priyom will run the sims and Rishi will draft the autoscale/burst policy language we put into the activation SOW.

Thanks — happy to jump back on a quick screen-share if helpful.

— Ava
Ava Chen
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0182

Attachments: edgehealth_capacity_modeling.xlsx (workbook), ops-ownership-matrix.csv (proposed owners + SLAs)

From: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: procure@edgehealth.ai
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Ava —

Appreciate the note and the artifacts. Quick answers to your asks:
- 30d forecast: baseline ~180 concurrent gen requests, expected short spikes up to ~1,100 concurrent during product marketing pushes (10–15 minute spikes, ~3–4x baseline).
- Top three windows: July 14 09:00–11:00 PT (launch), Aug 2 16:00–17:00 PT (partner webinar), Sept 21 08:00–12:00 PT (batch migration).
- p90 sequence length: ~1,200 tokens; p99 ~2,800 tokens. Streaming is ~35% of requests.
- Preferred primary region: us-west1; we can accept failover to us-east1 but prefer to avoid cross-region for sensitive workloads.
- Procurement: looped in (procure@edgehealth.ai).

We’re leaning toward Scenario A for predictability, but curious about the cost delta if we include a small multi-region spill window for >5min sustained spikes.

Jordan
Jordan Liu
Head of Platform, EdgeHealth
jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai
+1 206-555-0199

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 10:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Thanks Jordan — good numbers. I ran quick sims in the attached workbook with the inputs you gave (see tab "3x spike sim"). Short summary:

- Scenario A (Reserved + Burst): baseline pool sized at 200 concurrent GPUs (to comfortably serve 180 steady-state with room for cache/KV). Add a burst band of 800 concurrent for the 10–15 minute spikes. With pre-warm (30m prior) and admission control, median token cost increase ~18% over baseline but tail latency stays under p99 target for 95% of spikes.

- Scenario B (Smaller baseline + Multi-region spill): baseline 120 concurrent + auto-spill to us-east1 for sustained spikes >5 minutes. Cost baseline lower by ~12% but operational risk increases: cross-region KV consistency, cold-starts in secondary region add ~1.2–1.5s tail latency on first megacache misses.

My recommendation: proceed with Scenario A for initial activation to meet SLAs, but include a gated multi-region spill plan as an opt-in for specific event windows (we can enable per-campaign). That gives EdgeHealth predictability and the option to save costs when appropriate.

Notes on autoscale policy (draft):
- Warm-up: pre-warm for scheduled events (T-30m) via short-lived pre-provision calls.
- Burst admission: queue + probed admission for requests exceeding baseline until warmed nodes return to service.
- Max burst window: 20 minutes unconstrained; beyond that, we either escalate to multi-region spill or trigger manual approval to expand reserved pool.

I attached the ops-ownership matrix with suggested day-to-day responsibilities: EdgeHealth SRE owns traffic shaping rules and feature flags for pre-warm, Redwood owns underlying node health and kernel-level fallbacks + 24/7 on-call escalation.

— Priyom
Priyom Das
Platform Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priyom.das@redwood.ai

> On Tue, Jun 30, 2026 at 10:05 AM Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai> wrote:
> Ava —
> 
> Appreciate the note and the artifacts. Quick answers to your asks: ...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>, procure@edgehealth.ai
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 11:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

FWIW from commercial/procurement side:
- Lead time for adding reserved GPUs to a committed pool is ~4–6 weeks depending on region capacity. For a go-live aligned to a launch (e.g., July 14), we need a signed addendum by July 7 to guarantee pre-warm windows.
- Pricing: Scenario A (reserved + burst) will have a higher committed cost but predictable unit economics; we can include a one-time activation credit for the first major event to offset warm-up inefficiency — I’ll propose a 10% activation credit in the SOW.
- DocuSign: I’ve prepared an MSA addendum draft that inserts the explicit autoscale/burst policy language Priyom outlined. Sign here: https://www.docusign.com/sign/EDGE-7421-addendum.

Operational ownership language (draft):
- EdgeHealth SRE: feature-flagged pre-warm scheduling, confirmation of traffic windows, and local routing overrides.
- Redwood: provisioning, node lifecycle, kernel/fallback engineering; Redwood 24/7 on-call for infrastructure incidents.
- Escalation path: EdgeHealth first-page pager -> Redwood infra on-call (30 min SLA) -> Redwood ENG escalation (2 hours).

Next steps:
1) Jordan/procurement to sign addendum by 2026-07-07 to secure July 14 pre-warm.
2) Priyom to finalize sim outputs and commit sizes by EOD July 2.
3) Rishi to translate policy language into SOW clause and attach to addendum.

I’ll open a HubSpot activity and attach the SOW draft (link added to the thread).

— Marissa
Marissa Cole
Director, Revenue Operations, Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

Related HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/7421

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Thanks all — short recap/acceptance so we have a single source of truth:
- Decision: Proceed with Scenario A (Reserved baseline + defined burst band) as primary activation approach.
- Baseline commit: 200 concurrent (Redwood to provision in us-west1).
- Burst band: 800 concurrent for short spikes (pre-warm 30m before scheduled events).
- Multi-region spill: Enabled as an opt-in for specific events only (manual enable by EdgeHealth SRE + Redwood confirmation).
- Ownership: EdgeHealth SRE owns scheduling/traffic flags; Redwood owns provisioned nodes and 24/7 infra on-call. Escalation path as Marissa outlined.

Action items assigned:
- Jordan / procurement: sign the addendum by 2026-07-07 to guarantee July 14 pre-warm (procure@edgehealth.ai).
- Priyom: finalize sims and deliver final workbook tab by 2026-07-02.
- Rishi: draft autoscale/burst clause for SOW and upload to DocuSign by 2026-07-02.
- Marissa: attach SOW to HubSpot deal and start addendum workflow.

Subject: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Hi Jordan —

Thanks again for the run-through yesterday. To make the dedicated capacity plan concrete for your SRE and procurement teams, I attached two artifacts: a capacity-modeling workbook (edgehealth_capacity_modeling.xlsx) and a proposed ops-ownership matrix (ops-ownership-matrix.csv).

As discussed, we want to align on:
- Peak concurrency and expected burst patterns (typical business hours vs promo windows)
- Target latency SLOs (p50/p90/p99 tail targets and acceptable degradation under burst)
- Region preference for primary capacity and acceptable failover region(s)
- Preferred burst mechanism: reserved capacity + controlled burst vs ephemeral multi-region spillover
- Who owns day-to-day pool lifecycle (EdgeHealth SRE vs Redwood CS/ops) and on-call escalation path

Quick asks for your team so we can finalize the provisioning plan and commit timelines:
1) Confirm a 30d forecast for concurrent requests and a list of 3 expected high-traffic windows (dates/times).
2) Provide p90 sequence length estimate (tokens) and percent of requests using streaming vs non-streaming generation.
3) Confirm preferred primary region (us-west1 / us-east1 / europe-west1) and whether cross-region failover is allowed.
4) Introduce procurement contact (procure@edgehealth.ai is fine) so Marissa can start lead-time discussions.

We’ll run two provisioning scenarios in the workbook:
- Scenario A (Reserved + Burst): baseline reserved pool sized for steady-state + a defined burst band that pre-warms nodes and uses queued admission for short spikes.
- Scenario B (Multi-region spill): smaller baseline but with automated spill to secondary region when latency breach is imminent.

If you can reply with the numbers above by EOD Friday (July 2), Priyom will run the sims and Rishi will draft the autoscale/burst policy language we put into the activation SOW.

Thanks — happy to jump back on a quick screen-share if helpful.

— Ava

Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.ai
+1 415-555-0182

Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Ava —

Appreciate the note and the artifacts. Quick answers to your asks:
- 30d forecast: baseline ~180 concurrent gen requests, expected short spikes up to ~1,100 concurrent during product marketing pushes (10–15 minute spikes, ~3–4x baseline).
- Top three windows: July 14 09:00–11:00 PT (launch), Aug 2 16:00–17:00 PT (partner webinar), Sept 21 08:00–12:00 PT (batch migration).
- p90 sequence length: ~1,200 tokens; p99 ~2,800 tokens. Streaming is ~35% of requests.
- Preferred primary region: us-west1; we can accept failover to us-east1 but prefer to avoid cross-region for sensitive workloads.
- Procurement: looped in (procure@edgehealth.ai).

We’re leaning toward Scenario A for predictability, but curious about the cost delta if we include a small multi-region spill window for >5min sustained spikes.

Jordan

Head of Platform, EdgeHealth

From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Liu <jordan.liu@edgehealth.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 10:42:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: EdgeHealth capacity surge scenarios & ops ownership agreement

Thanks Jordan — good numbers. I ran quick sims in the attached workbook with the inputs you gave (see tab "3x spike sim"). Short summary:

- Scenario A (Reserved + Burst): baseline pool sized at 200 concurrent GPUs (to comfortably serve 180 steady-state with room for cache/KV). Add a burst band of 800 concurrent for the 10–15 minute spikes. With pre-warm (30m prior) and admission control, median token cost increase ~18% over baseline but tail latency stays under p99 target for 95% of spikes.

- Scenario B (Smaller baseline + Multi-region spill): baseline 120 concurrent + auto-spill to us-east1 for sustained spikes >5 minutes. Cost baseline lower by ~12% but operational risk increases: cross-region KV consistency, cold-starts in secondary region add ~1.2–1.5s tail latency on first megacache misses.

My recommendation: proceed with Scenario A for initial activation to meet SLAs, but include a gated multi-region spill plan as an opt-in for specific event windows (we can enable per-campaign). That gives EdgeHealth predictability and the option to save costs when appropriate.

Notes on autoscale policy (draft):
- Warm-up: pre-warm for scheduled events (T-30m) via short-lived pre-provision calls.
- Burst admission: queue + probed admission for requests exceeding baseline until warmed nodes return to service.
- Max burst window: 20 minutes unconstrained; beyond that, we either escalate to multi-region spill or trigger manual approval to expand reserved pool.

I attached the ops-ownership matrix with suggested day-to-day responsibilities: EdgeHealth SRE owns traffic shaping rules and feature flags for pre-warm, Redwood owns underlying node health and kernel-level fallbacks + 24/7 on-call escalation.

— Priyom
Priyom Das
Platform Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priyom.das@redwood.ai

title: AZ1 traffic diversion approved — Acme escalation
preview:
From: Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@acme-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:05:00 +0000
Subject: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

Ben / team,

We're seeing a sustained spike in 5xx errors from requests routed to AZ1 (eastus-az1) beginning ~14:50Z. Error rate is ~28–32% for customer-facing generation endpoints and median P95 latency has gone from ~120ms to ~650–800ms. Traffic volume is normal; this is causing timeouts in our ingestion pipeline and a handful of production layoffs for two key customers.

We need an immediate mitigation plan and confirmation you can divert traffic to a different region (us-east-2 or eu-west-1) for the affected customers. Can you confirm what's happening on your side and whether a regional switchover is possible without rolling back model versions? We also need an ETA for a fix and a timeline for a postmortem.

Attached: error_snapshot.json (sample requests/trace ids)

Ravi
Ops, Acme Cloud Services


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@acme-cloud.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:20:00 +0000
Subject: Re: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

Ravi — thanks for the flag and the snapshot. I'm escalating to SRE and the routing team now (cc'd).

Immediate plan I'm proposing:
- SRE to evaluate whether this is capacity/infra or model runtime (Jonas, Karthik)
- If infra-related, initiate a traffic diversion policy to move affected prefixes/customers to us-east-2 as a hot fallback (we have pre-warmed pools there)
- We'll start with a 20% canary for 10 minutes and ramp to 100% if errors drop below 2%

Can you confirm: which customer prefixes are impacted (the traces show 3 customer ids) and whether Acme approves immediate diversion? If yes, we'll execute within 15 minutes. We'll also open an exec-level channel if this affects SLAs.

Ben Carter
Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:27:00 +0000
Subject: Re: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

Ben / team — quick triage:
- Confirmed: elevated 5xx for the serving tier in us-east-1a (AZ1). Traces show repeated KV cache misses and TLS handshake retries between router and worker pools.
- Recent infra event: a noisy hypervisor node group triggered IO contention in the AZ; autoscaler partially replaced nodes but we saw degraded throughput for ~8 minutes starting 14:45Z.
- Model versions are healthy; this looks infra-level.

Recommended immediate steps (from runbook):
1) Apply traffic diversion policy: route traffic for impacted customer prefixes to us-east-2 using weight-based routing (20% -> monitor -> 100%).
2) Reduce TTL on route for the affected prefix to 30s to speed convergence.
3) Disable warmup throttling on the target pool to accept increased concurrency.

I attached a short timeline (timeline.csv) with traces and metric slices. I'll stand by to run the diversion and monitor token success-rate and P95 latency.

-Jonas
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:40:00 +0000
Subject: Re: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

Ben — operational plan I will execute on Jonas' signal:
- Step 0: capture live profiling and extend logging for the affected prefixes to 60s retention.
- Step 1: apply route change via control plane: set weights: AZ1 80% -> us-east-2 20% (canary), TTL=30s, health-check interval 15s
- Step 2: after 10 minutes, if success-rate > 98% and P95 latency < 250ms, ramp to 100% to us-east-2 and mark AZ1 as degraded for additional traffic until mitigation complete
- Step 3: notify AWS AM to accelerate infra replacement (cc Maria Santos?)

CLI snippet: 
redwood route update --prefix acme-prod-1 --weights us-east-1a:80,us-east-2:20 --ttl 30 --health 15

I will gate the final ramp on metric checks and keep the customer updated.

-Karthik
Lead Infra Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@acme-cloud.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Maria Santos <maria.santos@aws-partner.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 15:45:00 +0000
Subject: FW: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

Ravi — per SRE, the issue is infra-level in us-east-1a. We have a pre-authorized mitigation path for production customers on the account and propose to do the following immediately on your approval:
- Start traffic diversion to us-east-2: 20% canary now -> 100% in up to 15 minutes if metrics look good
- Monitor success-rate, latency, and cost delta in real time; we'll share a short live dashboard link.

If you approve, reply \"APPROVE DIVERSION\" and we'll start the canary. We'll also prepare a postmortem and work with Maria (AWS) to get root cause and replacement ETA. We will discuss SLA credits in parallel — our CS lead will follow up.

Attachments: traffic_diversion_runbook.pdf (operational checklist)

Ben


From: Ravi Menon <ravi.menon@acme-cloud.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>, Maria Santos <maria.santos@aws-partner.com>
Date: Tue, 30 Jun 2026 16:02:00 +0000
Subject: Re: High error rate + latency from AZ1 — immediate attention

APPROVE DIVERSION — please proceed.

Initial check: after the first 10% of traffic moved, error-rate dropped to ~3% and P95 latency to ~180ms. Please keep us posted on full ramp and supply a postmortem and SLA credit timeline. We'll notify our internal execs but appreciate rapid action here.

Ravi
Ops, Acme Cloud Services

title: MAP: Verification Kanban + Exit Tests — Acme Health
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-06-25T09:12:00Z
Subject: MAP: Verification Kanban + Exit Tests — Acme Health

Jane —

Great to meet last week. Per our conversation I've attached a draft MAP (MAP-v0.3.xlsx) that focuses exclusively on verification activities we agreed were critical for the trial: instrumentation, smoke test harness, and the go/no-go exit checks.

Can you confirm the acceptance band for the primary objectives below so we can lock the runbook? If these look right we'll convert them into the Kanban and assign owners this week.

Primary success criteria (proposed):
- Latency: 95th percentile inference latency < 350ms for 1–2 sentence prompts (sample workload)
- Accuracy / correctness: 85% pass rate on the blinded sample of 200 prompts (see sample set tab)
- Availability: 99.5% service availability during trial window
- Data handling: no customer PII persisted in our logs; access controls validated

Proposed exit gates (examples):
1) Smoke-green: End-to-end demo + 50-sample concordance >= 90% -> progress to canary
2) Canary-green: 7-day canary with 95th pctl latency and stability hold -> handover to CS
3) Blocker: any security compliance item marked P1 by Acme's team -> pause and remediation

Quick asks:
1) Confirm the acceptance bands above or share alternate thresholds
2) Share any mandatory compliance evidence items beyond the checklist we discussed
3) Preferred signoff owners at Acme (technical and procurement) so we can build the mutual action flow

Attachments: MAP-v0.3.xlsx (kanban + test matrix)

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter
Principal Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123



From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@acmehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Date: 2026-06-26T10:05:00Z
Subject: Re: MAP: Verification Kanban + Exit Tests — Acme Health

Ben —

Thanks for the draft. Quick responses inline (and attaching our security checklist):

> Primary success criteria (proposed):
> - Latency: 95th percentile inference latency < 350ms
> - Accuracy / correctness: 85% pass rate on the blinded sample of 200 prompts
> - Availability: 99.5% service availability during trial window
> - Data handling: no customer PII persisted in our logs; access controls validated

Those look reasonable as starting points. A couple of constraints from our side:
- Data residency: All inference logs must remain in the us-east-1 region for the scope of the POC.
- Evidence: for the data handling ask, we need an audit log sample and confirmation of KMS key handling for transient caches.

Acceptance bands we need to lock before kickoff:
1) Accuracy: 80% pass required for demo -> if we miss by more than 5 points we require remediation sprint
2) Latency: if 95th pctl spikes above 500ms for > 4 hours we will treat as a performance incident

Signoff owners:
- Technical: me (Jane Doe, Dir. Clinical Ops) and Rajesh Patel (Acme infra lead)
- Procurement: procurement@acmehealth.com (PO will be issued after technical signoff)

We'll review the MAP-v0.3.xlsx in detail and return with a marked-up kanban by Tuesday.

Regards,
Jane

title: RTBF removal SLA options & backup retention edge cases
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elaine Smith <elaine.smith@datavault.io>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: RTBF / data removal discussion — call recap and clarifying Qs

Hi Elaine / Datavault team,

Thanks again for taking the time today. Quick recap from our discovery call and a few clarifying questions so we can scope the MAP and any legal items: 

1) What we heard from your side
- Customer-facing app stores user-generated content (text + occasional file upload) for up to 2 years.
- Customers may submit RTBF requests tied to an account ID or email address.
- You need a documented, auditable flow that proves deletion and the timeline for all persisted copies.

2) Clarifying questions
- Active data vs. backups: you mentioned backups are retained for \"~90 days\" in your current policy. Can you confirm whether backups are considered immutable snapshots (i.e., cannot be modified) and if they are treated as a separate retention silo for compliance requests?
- Logs & analytics: which logs do you consider PII (access logs, API request bodies)? Are there any regulatory rules (GDPR articles / contractual) that force immediate log deletion vs. pseudonymization?
- Deletion triggers: do you prefer a self-service API for data deletion requests, or would you route deletion requests via your legal team and then call our deletion endpoint?

3) Redwood capabilities (high level)
- We can execute logical deletes within 24–72 hours for active data and provide an expunge job for KV cache + indexes.
- Backups are handled by our infra partner in the region; backup snapshots can be flagged for expedited removal but may require a manual purge process and an audit window.

Attached: redwood-rtbf-options.pdf — lightweight options matrix (immediate logical delete, deferred purge, backup-expedite SLA).

Next steps 
- Can you confirm the backup retention classification (mutable vs immutable) and which region(s) this applies to?
- If possible, share a brief sample of the deletion request workflow (who approves, ticketing flow).

I’ll loop in Markus and Monica for MAP/contract alignment once we have those answers. Proposed follow-up: Tue 30 Jun, 10am PT — same meeting invite?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

Attachments: redwood-rtbf-options.pdf (13 KB)


From: Elaine Smith <elaine.smith@datavault.io>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: support@datavault.io
Date: Fri, 26 Jun 2026 11:03:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Follow-up: RTBF / data removal discussion — call recap and clarifying Qs

Hi Ben — thanks for the write-up, this is helpful. Answers inline below.

On Wed, Jun 24, 2026 at 9:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> 1) What we heard from your side
> - Customer-facing app stores user-generated content (text + occasional file upload) for up to 2 years.
> - Customers may submit RTBF requests tied to an account ID or email address.
> - You need a documented, auditable flow that proves deletion and the timeline for all persisted copies.

Correct. A couple of important details: certain enterprise customers require a 180-day hold window for investigatory reasons — we'll flag those as exception cases.

> 2) Clarifying questions
> - Active data vs. backups: you mentioned backups are retained for \"~90 days\" in your current policy. Can you confirm whether backups are considered immutable snapshots (i.e., cannot be modified) and if they are treated as a separate retention silo for compliance requests?

Backups are immutable snapshots in our current infra. We have a process to isolate and flag snapshots for deletion, but it is a manual workflow due to our backup provider SLA. For now plan on a 90-day backup availability and a separate manual purge that can be escalated — typical purge lead time is 21–45 days after a signed deletion request.

> - Logs & analytics: which logs do you consider PII (access logs, API request bodies)? Are there any regulatory rules (GDPR articles / contractual) that force immediate log deletion vs. pseudonymization?

We classify API request bodies as sensitive when they contain user identifiers or content flagged as PII. Access logs (IP, timestamp) are kept 180 days in aggregate; we can pseudonymize IPs on request but full removal is more complicated because of forensic needs for fraud teams.

> - Deletion triggers: do you prefer a self-service API for data deletion requests, or would you route deletion requests via your legal team and then call our deletion endpoint?

Legal-managed flow for enterprise customers; self-service for consumer tenants. We expect a signed \"deletion confirmation\" from customers for enterprise requests.

We attached our internal RTBF overview for reference (datavault-rtbf-overview.docx).

Let us know whether Redwood can provide an \"expedite\" code path for backup purges (we would pay for the work if needed). Also: confirm whether your logs retention can be configured per-customer/tenant — that’s a hard requirement for one of our enterprise pilots.

Best,
Elaine
Director, Security & Privacy | Datavault, Inc.
elaine.smith@datavault.io

title: MAP weekly checkpoint — integration ops bridge (SDK / auth / streaming / tool-calls)
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@ariacorp.com>, Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@ariacorp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 09:00:00 -0700
Subject: MAP weekly checkpoint — integration ops bridge (SDK / auth / streaming / tool-calls)

Hi Elena / Jordan / team,

Thanks for joining the MAP. Quick written checkpoint from our side to capture owners, current status, and blockers on the integration path to close the POC and align the eval plan timeline. Attached: MAP_v1.2.xlsx (action tracker) and eval_plan.yaml (test vectors & success criteria).

High level status
- Goal: close integration and complete evaluation run by 2026-07-12.
- Overall progress: 55% complete on technical integrations, eval plan in draft, two high-priority blockers.

Completed since last week
- Auth spec: Redwoods' JWT-based scoped tokens validated against Aria testbed (Monica).
- Initial streaming handshake verified end-to-end in staging (Marissa).

In progress / next steps
- SDK token refresh: implement automatic refresh + backoff behavior (owner: Monica) — target: 2026-06-28 EOD.
- Tool calling scope mapping: finalize allowlist for Aria tools (owner: Jordan / Aria) — target: 2026-07-02.
- Streaming keep-alive and reconnect behavior: finalize edge handling (owner: Marissa) — target: 2026-07-05.

Blockers
- Short-lived presigned URLs generated by Aria proxy are expiring before SDK refresh cycle; need a coordinated window or extend TTLs to >= 5 minutes. (Action: Aria network / infra)
- One eval vector (large context tool-call) needs a deterministic fallback — tracking separately in eval_plan.yaml.

Action items (short list)
1) Monica Patel — push SDK token refresh patch to PR and publish nightly build (due: 2026-06-28 EOD) [attachment: sdk-token-flow.png].
2) Jordan Miles (Aria) — increase presigned URL TTL or provide refresh endpoint for proxy (due: 2026-06-29).
3) Marissa Cole — close streaming reconnect test cases and publish results (due: 2026-07-05).
4) Ben Carter / Karthik Iyer — finalize commercial schedule draft for eval run (due: 2026-07-01).

Please reply with any corrections to owners or timelines. If helpful, propose a 15-min sync this afternoon to unblock the presigned URL TTL question.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Lead| Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0123

Attachments: MAP_v1.2.xlsx (action tracker), eval_plan.yaml (eval vectors)


From: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@ariacorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Miles <jordan.miles@ariacorp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP weekly checkpoint — integration ops bridge (SDK / auth / streaming / tool-calls)

Ben — thanks for the clear summary. A few clarifications from our side: 

- Presigned URL TTL: we can extend to 10 minutes for the testbed. Production proxy will need a refresh endpoint; I'll spin up a ticket and assign to our infra lead (cc'd Jordan).
- SDK refresh behavior: can you confirm if the SDK will attempt a refresh only after a 401, or proactively before expiry? We saw a case where a long-running streaming session hit a 401 mid-stream and the client did not recover without full reconnect.

On tool-calling scope: Aria's security policy requires minimize-permission principle for calls that access billing or PII systems; ideally scope tokens to a named tool list. We'll provide the allowlist by 2026-06-30.

Small ask: could Redwood share the exact token lifetimes you're issuing in staging? A short snippet in the token payload will help our infra simulate expiry.

Thanks — Elena
Elena Rossi
Head of R&D, AriaCorp
elena.rossi@ariacorp.com


From: Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.com>
To: Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@ariacorp.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 12:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP weekly checkpoint — integration ops bridge (SDK / auth / streaming / tool-calls)

On Sun, Jun 28, 2026 at 10:45 AM Elena Rossi <elena.rossi@ariacorp.com> wrote:
> - SDK refresh behavior: can you confirm if the SDK will attempt a refresh only after a 401, or proactively before expiry?

Good question — implemented behavior in the current SDK build (nightly) is proactive: it checks token expiry and refreshes when remaining lifetime < 60s. For streaming sessions we have an internal keepalive that triggers a refresh preemptively to avoid mid-stream 401s. That said, we reproduced the reconnect case you saw when presigned URLs expired outside the token lifecycle.

Plan: I opened PR #42 with an adjusted backoff (proactive refresh window to 90s) and an additional reconnect flow that attempts a soft resume before a full reconnect. PR link: https://github.com/redwood-inference/aria-integ/pull/42.

I'll publish a nightly build with the change (tag: aria-integ-2026-06-28) and attach a token flow diagram. If Aria can extend TTLs to 5+ min in staging that should cover most edge cases while we validate the new SDK behavior.

Will follow up once build is published.

— Monica
Monica Patel
Integration Engineer | Redwood Inference
monica_patel@redwood.com
Attachment: sdk-token-flow.png

- Change payment terms from Net-30 to Net-75

Suggested trade-offs we could consider:

3) Limit the concession to a 12-month term; revert to standard billing cadence after renewal unless explicitly re-approved.

Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 09:48:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Team — quick finance read.

Net-75 + quarterly invoicing increases DSO risk materially. With a $420k ARR figure, this could push receivables out by >60 days vs. Net-30. That said, the 25% upfront significantly mitigates exposure. If we do this, I recommend: 

- 25% prepayment at SOW signature (recognize per ASC guidance on delivery terms)
- Quarterly invoices for the balance with Net-60 as a preference (Net-75 only if we include a 2% early-payment discount or other credit protection)
- Add a clause allowing us to invoice immediately for any professional services or onboarding expenses to improve cashflow.

I've attached a proposed invoice schedule (shows timing and expected cashflow) and created BILL-2117 to track accounting changes and revenue recognition notes. Legal should sign off on the redline Ben attached.

If you want to escalate, Vivek — Ben is asking for exec approval for the Net-75 concession tied to the prepayment and discount.

-Marissa

[Attachment: invoice_schedule_qhealth.xlsx]
[Related ticket: https://jira.internal.redwood/browse/BILL-2117]


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 29 Jun 2026 15:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042) — Exec decision

Ben / Marissa / Ava — after reviewing the trade-offs I'm comfortable approving a limited concession to accommodate Quadrant, given the strategic value in Healthcare. Approvals and conditions below: 

- Approve: Quarterly invoicing AND Net-75 on quarterly invoices.
- Conditions:
  1) 25% prepayment at contract signing, applied to the first quarter invoice.
  2) 2% early-payment discount (applies if Quadrant pays any invoice within 30 days).
  3) Concession limited to the initial 12-month term; renewal to default to Net-30 and monthly invoicing unless re-approved.
  4) Legal to add language for milestone invoicing (no more than 2 splits per quarter) and a clause allowing Redwood to accelerate invoices in case of missed payments.

Ava — please capture this as an amendment and route via DocuSign. Marissa, please confirm accounting entries for the prepayment and that BILL-2117 is assigned to revenue operations. Ben — run the concession as a deal note in HubSpot (link below) and ensure procurement memo is attached.

Thanks — Vivek

[Action: Ava to send DocuSign amendment 
DocuSign prep: https://docusign.com/prepare/HQ-2026-042]

title: MAP: performance validation plan and emb + rerank load verification
preview:
From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Sat, 20 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Hi Elena / Carlos —

Attaching an initial Mutual Action Plan (MAP) and a proposed performance validation plan for the embeddings + reranker endpoints. High-level asks:

- Confirm dataset and tokenization expectations (we need a representative query set, sample size ~12k queries).
- Confirm test windows on your side (we'll need a 4-hour window for the full concurrency sweep).
- Agree targets: 95th p99 latency under 350ms for embeddings (per vector), reranker TTFT p95 < 700ms @ 8 concurrent requests, and sustained throughput targets (tokens/s).

What we've attached: searchly_map_perf_plan.xlsx (MAP + timeline + responsibilities), and a short test harness note. The plan includes an incremental approach: smoke -> controlled concurrency sweep -> sustained 1-hour soak. We will run on Redwood Dedicated test pool that mirrors your prod-ish GPU type (A100 equivalent).

If you confirm the dataset and a tentative test window next week we can lock the schedule and execute a P0 run. Goal from Sales side: have performance artifacts for internal sign-off and for procurement by 2026-07-08.

Thanks —
Karthik
Head of Solutions, Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai
Redwood Inference

Attachment: searchly_map_perf_plan.xlsx (MAP and perf plan)


From: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Sun, 21 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Thanks Karthik — this looks good as a starting point. A few clarifications from our side:

1) Dataset: we'll produce the 12k query bundle (searchly_test_dataset_v3.zip) and drop a link into the shared doc by Tuesday EOD. It contains anonymized user queries + groundtruth relevancy labels for the reranker.
2) Parity: we need to confirm that the Dedicated test pool uses the same sequence length caps as our prod model (max 1024 tokens) — can you confirm?
3) Concurrency: happy to run the 4-hour window; please include an extra run at 50 concurrent sessions for peak tail behavior.

We can aim for the test window on Wed 24th. CC'ing Carlos to confirm procurement timelines.

—Elena
Product Ops, Searchly
elena@searchly.com


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 14:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: MAP draft + perf plan for emb + rerank benchmarks

Elena — quick logistics from the bench team:

- Environment: Redwood Dedicated (test pool) is configured with NVidia A100-s equivalent, sequence length cap set to 1024 (confirmed). Tokenizer parity: we'll use the same tokenizer binary you provide.
- Harness: running redwood-bench v2.1 + our JMeter wrapper (emb_rerank_load_script.jmx attached). The harness records latency (p50/p95/p99), TTFT (time to first token for rerank streaming), per-request tokens generated, and throughput (tokens/sec).
- Test matrix (summary):
  * Smoke: 5 concurrent -> verify correctness
  * Sweep: 1,2,4,8,16,32,50 concurrency (ramp 1m steps)
  * Soak: 60 minutes at target concurrency (16 and 50 runs)

Targets we recommend (for MAP acceptance):
- Embeddings: median < 40ms, p95 < 120ms
- Reranker (single-pass, no external sidecars): TTFT p50 < 220ms, p95 < 700ms; sustained throughput >= 140 requests/min at 8 concurrency (proxy for production QPS).

I'll run a smoke on Tuesday morning and share baseline artifacts (logs + CSV). If anything looks off we can re-run with tuned batching/quantization flags.

-Marissa
Perf Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: emb_rerank_load_script.jmx


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>
Cc: Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 24 Jun 2026 11:47:00 -0700
Subject: Interim run results: emb + rerank (summary)

Elena / team — ran the controlled sweep this morning (dataset subset ~3k queries) and attached the CSV with raw samples (emb_rerank_load_report.csv). High-level summary below:

Embeddings endpoint (single-vector calls)\\- Concurrency 1: p50 18ms, p95 42ms, throughput 750 vectors/s\\- Concurrency 8: p50 22ms, p95 110ms, throughput 5.6k vectors/s\\- Concurrency 50: p50 48ms, p95 190ms, throughput 18.2k vectors/s

Reranker endpoint (ranker returns top-K, streaming first token)\\- Concurrency 1: TTFT p50 140ms, p95 295ms, throughput 18 req/s\\- Concurrency 8: TTFT p50 210ms, p95 610ms, throughput 128 req/min\\- Concurrency 50: TTFT p50 420ms, p95 1320ms, throughput 720 req/hr

Notes:
- The 50-concurrency run shows p95 degradation for reranker (1.3s). This looks related to CPU-bound postprocessing after the model pass (we're capturing host metrics). We can mitigate via a short-term worker scaling tweak in the mapping, or use an optimized postproc binary.
- Embeddings stayed well within MAP targets up to 32 conc. At 50 we see higher p95 but median remains acceptable.

Next steps suggested:
1) Optional targeted rerun at 50 with scaled workers (30m) to confirm remediation.
2) Full soak (60m) at 16 concurrency to verify stability.
3) Final artifacts + MAP update for sales sign-off.

Proposed close timeline: if we confirm remediation on the 50-concurrency test and soak is green, we can produce MAP final artifacts and sign by 2026-07-08.

Thanks —
Monica Patel
Customer Reliability Eng, Redwood Inference
Attachment: emb_rerank_load_report.csv


From: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 Jun 2026 09:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Interim run results: emb + rerank (summary)

Thanks Monica — useful breakdown. A couple of quick questions:

1) For parity validation, can you confirm the tokenizer binary version used and the exact model sha/tag? We need that recorded for our performance compliance packet.
2) Please run the 50-concurrency test again with the proposed worker scaling change and share logs. Preferably keep the same dataset slice so we can compare apples-to-apples.

We'll upload the dataset bundle to the shared Drive and the procurement team (Carlos) will follow up on the standard SOW changes if there are any adjustments.

Attaching: searchly_test_dataset_v3.zip (link in shared doc).

—Elena


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Park <elena@searchly.com>, Carlos Mendez <carlos@searchly.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Sean Gallagher <sean@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 26 Jun 2026 15:03:00 -0700

Elena / Carlos — thanks for the fast turnaround and for preparing the dataset. Quick summary and proposed commitments so we have a single source of truth in the MAP: 

Action items (Redwood):
- Re-run 50-concurrency reranker with scaled workers today (Monica / Marissa) and share logs & CSV within 24 hours.
- Full 60-minute soak at 16 concurrency on Friday 2026-06-28 (Monica).
- Produce a signed MAP artifact + perf annex (includes p50/p95/p99 tables, traces, host metrics) once runs are green; target MAP sign-off by 2026-07-08.

Action items (Searchly):
- Drop final dataset bundle and tokenizer binary in the shared doc (Elena) by 2026-06-27 EOD.
- Procurement (Carlos) to review proposed SOW tweak for additional worker scaling and confirm by 2026-07-01.

Acceptance criteria for MAP close:
- Embeddings: p95 <= 120ms (validated across 1..32 conc), TTFT for reranker p95 <= 700ms at 8 conc, and documented mitigation plan for 50-concurrency behavior with observed post-run artifacts.

Links to artifacts: HubSpot deal: https://hubspot.example.com/deal/SLY-4721 ; MAP doc: https://docs.redwood.ai/shared/searchly-map-spec-v1 ; DocuSign quote: https://app.docusign.com/SLY-4721/quote

If this matches your expectations I will update the MAP spreadsheet and move to the procurement thread for SOW formalization.

Thanks —
Karthik

Embeddings endpoint (single-vector calls)\\- Concurrency 1: p50 18ms, p95 42ms, throughput 750 vectors/s\\- Concurrency 8: p50 22ms, p95 110ms, throughput 5.6k vectors/s\\- Concurrency 50: p50 48ms, p95 190ms, throughput 18.2k vectors/s

Reranker endpoint (ranker returns top-K, streaming first token)\\- Concurrency 1: TTFT p50 140ms, p95 295ms, throughput 18 req/s\\- Concurrency 8: TTFT p50 210ms, p95 610ms, throughput 128 req/min\\- Concurrency 50: TTFT p50 420ms, p95 1320ms, throughput 720 req/hr

Notes:
- The 50-concurrency run shows p95 degradation for reranker (1.3s). This looks related to CPU-bound postprocessing after the model pass (we're capturing host metrics). We can mitigate via a short-term worker scaling tweak in the mapping, or use an optimized postproc binary.
- Embeddings stayed well within MAP targets up to 32 conc. At 50 we see higher p95 but median remains acceptable.

Next steps suggested:
1) Optional targeted rerun at 50 with scaled workers (30m) to confirm remediation.
2) Full soak (60m) at 16 concurrency to verify stability.
3) Final artifacts + MAP update for sales sign-off.

Proposed close timeline: if we confirm remediation on the 50-concurrency test and soak is green, we can produce MAP final artifacts and sign by 2026-07-08.

Thanks —
Monica Patel
Customer Reliability Eng, Redwood Inference

title: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing, BAA milestones & evidence request
preview:
From: Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Hi Alex,

Thanks for the quick onboarding so far. As part of our procurement packet for CareBridge’s telehealth platform evaluation, our security & compliance team has a few clarifying questions around PHI handling and the BAA timeline before we can proceed to contract review: 

- Primary question: Can you confirm the available deployment modes that will see PHI? We need details about Hosted (shared infra), Dedicated, and Private (VPC/on-prem) options and the default isolation guarantees for each.
- Key management: Does Redwood support customer-managed keys (CMK) for encryption at rest? If so, what KMS integrations and key rotation controls are supported?
- Audit logs & exports: What audit events are captured (API calls, user access, model inference trace) and can we forward logs to our Splunk instance?
- Data residency & retention: Can you support configurable retention policies for PHI (we expect up to 7 years for some records)?
- Evidence: Please attach SOC 2 type II report (redacted if necessary), dataflow diagram for PHI, and a list of controls used to de-identify or minimize PHI in inference workloads.

We’ve attached the RFP (CareBridge_RFP_Q1.pdf) with these items enumerated. We’d also like an anticipated timeline for a draft BAA from Redwood legal — ideally within five business days if feasible.

Can we schedule a 30-min clarifying call this week? Our preferred times are 6/22 morning PST.

Thanks,
Jamie Li
Senior Procurement Manager
CareBridge Health

---
[Attachment: CareBridge_RFP_Q1.pdf]

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>
To: Jamie Li <j.li@carebridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing and evidence request

Hi Jamie — appreciate the clear list. Short answers up front, details below and attachments included.

- Deployment modes: Redwood supports Hosted API (multi-tenant), Dedicated capacity (single-tenant GPU pools), and Private (VPC-hosted control plane or on-prem package). PHI can be processed in any of these modes; for strongest isolation we recommend Dedicated or Private.
- KMS/CMK: Customer-managed keys are supported for Dedicated and Private. Integrations: AWS KMS, GCP KMS, and Azure Key Vault. We also support BYOK workflows for the on-prem package.
- Audit logs: We capture inference request metadata, authentication events, administrative actions, model version rollouts, and key-access logs (where integrated). Logs can be forwarded to customer SIEMs (Splunk, Datadog) via our secure log-forwarding endpoint.
- Retention: Retention is configurable per-route; we can set PHI retention windows up to 7 years and configure automatic purge policies.
- Evidence: SOC 2 Type II (redacted) plus a PHI dataflow diagram and a summary of de-identification controls attached.

I’ve attached: redwood_security_summary.pdf and redwood_dataflow.svg.

Timeline: legal can provide an initial BAA draft within five business days after we confirm the CMK option and retention window; we can then iterate quickly.

I’m available for a call 6/22 9:00–11:00am PST. If that works, I’ll calendar-in with Marissa and Monica.

Best,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

---
[Attachments: redwood_security_summary.pdf, redwood_dataflow.svg]

To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood-inference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood-inference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood-inference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T12:08:00-07:00

Jamie asked about CMK, 7-year retention, Splunk forwarding, and evidence. Action items: 
- Monica: can you confirm the exact set of audit events we can forward and any PII scrubbing options on the forwarded logs? Also confirm default retention config and steps to enable 7-year retention for a dedicated customer.
- Marcus: legal will want to know if CareBridge requires customer key custody for all environments or just Dedicated/Private. Advise on any technical gaps for Hosted + CMK.
- Alex: when you schedule the call, please include the Security Architecture doc link and ask them to indicate if they need an on-prem deployment questionnaire.

2) KMS: We prefer customer-managed keys for all PHI environments. Is the CMK model supported for your Hosted API, or limited to Dedicated/Private? If the Hosted API cannot support CMK, please propose compensating controls.

Date: 2026-06-25T15:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: CareBridge RFP — PHI routing, BAA milestones & evidence request

Hi Samuel / Jamie — summary and commitments below so counsel and engineering can proceed in parallel: 

1) Retention attestation: Redwood will provide a written attestation that retention windows are configurable per-route and that deletion follows irreversible purge workflows (we’ll include the purge operational runbook with the evidence package).
2) CMK support: CMK is supported for Dedicated and Private (VPC/on-prem) deployments. For Hosted API we do not support customer-controlled KMS today; compensating controls for Hosted include encryption-in-transit with TLS 1.2+, at-rest encryption with provider-managed KMS, strict tenant isolation policies, and optional request-level tokenization/prefix-caching to minimize persisted PHI. If CareBridge requires full customer key custody across all environments, we recommend Dedicated or Private.
3) Logs & Splunk: We can forward audit logs to your Splunk HTTP Event Collector endpoint. Logs are configurable — by default they do not include raw request payloads (customer can opt-in to include them). We will provide a log schema and redaction controls.

BAA: Attached is a draft BAA (BAA_draft_redline_v1.pdf) in editable form; legal will accept redlines and aims to return a signed BAA within 2–3 business days after receiving your counsel’s changes.

- We’ll share the full evidence package (SOC 2 redacted, purge runbook, log schema) in the Drive link before EOD 6/26.
- Proposed call confirmed for 6/24 10:30am PST — calendar invite sent; please add any technical folks who will review the dataflow.

If there are any missing items in the evidence matrix, flag them and we’ll prioritize.

Best regards,
Alex Martinez
Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Anika Rao <anika.rao@medisyshealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: Operator witnessing tiers and evidence path for private deployment

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week. As we start to firm up the order form for the private (VPC) deployment, procurement and our security team asked for clarification on three items so we can scope the MSA exhibits correctly:

1) Operator witnessing options: we need discrete definitions for (a) remote witnessed access, (b) on‑site witnessed access, and (c) escrow‑lite (sealed snapshot stewarded by a third party). For each option we need expected timelines, who's responsible for witness coordination, and the minimum artifacts Medisys receives to validate operator capability.

2) Credential and KMS boundary: who retains custody of operator creds during a witnessed session? Can an operator be scoped to ephemeral credentials that expire at session close, and do we need a signed attestation?

3) Proof window and evidence delivery: procurement wants a sample exhibit describing the proof‑of‑access window (how long witnessing is available), what logs/artifacts are delivered, and retention limits.

I've attached our internal Evidence Request Form to show the artifacts we typically collect. Can you point us to the order‑form language or an exhibit draft we can share with legal/procurement? Also please include any incremental cost or scheduling impact for on‑site versus remote witnessed access.

Best,
Anika Rao
Director, IT Security — Medisys Health
Anika Rao
Attachment: Medisys_Evidence_Request_Form.pdf (pdf)


From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Anika Rao <anika.rao@medisyshealth.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa.morgan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-24T12:38:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Operator witnessing tiers and evidence path for private deployment

Hi Anika — really useful list, thanks. Looping in our legal and ops leads so we can put concrete language against each option. Quick notes up front:

- We typically offer two pragmatic patterns for customers wanting "operator proof":
  * Witnessed Access (Remote or On‑Site): a Redwood operator performs the required task while Medisys observers (remote screen share or on site). Operator uses ephemeral credentials for the session where feasible; logs, session video (if allowed), and signed session attestation are delivered.
  * Escrow‑Lite (Sealed Snapshot + Steward): Redwood produces a sealed snapshot of the deployed artifacts and deposits it with a neutral steward; steward verifies inventory and can provide witness access on contractually defined trigger events.

- Timeline: remote witnessed sessions — typically available within 2 weeks of scheduling; on‑site witness — 4–6 weeks (travel + facility access); escrow‑lite snapshot delivery — roughly 6–8 weeks to stage, verify, and steward.

I've attached a short guidance note that includes an annotated list of artifacts we can provide and a sample cost table. Lauren — can you review the proposed exhibit language below and suggest redlines (indemnity, operator credential scope, and retention caps)? Irene / Jordan — can you confirm ops runbook and evidence delivery format (file naming, hash, signed attestations)?

Next step: propose a 45‑minute workshop this week with security and procurement to agree the exhibit scope, then we'll circulate a draft Exhibit G for procurement review.

Available times: Thu 06/25 10:00–12:00 PT, Fri 06/26 09:00–11:00 PT — which works for you?

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Head of Field Sales, Redwood Inference
Attachment: redline_guidance.pdf (pdf)


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Cc: Anika Rao <anika.rao@medisyshealth.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-25T09:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Operator witnessing tiers and evidence path for private deployment

Alex / Anika — quick pass from legal with proposed drafting points. Below is concise, but happy to expand into Exhibit language. Quoting Alex's summary for context: 

> We typically offer two pragmatic patterns for customers wanting "operator proof": Witnessed Access (Remote or On‑Site) and Escrow‑Lite (Sealed Snapshot + Steward).

Proposed legal points (high level):

1) License and scope: Add a short exhibit that clarifies license residency (models may be resident in Customer VPC or in Redwood managed ephemeral instances), and that operator actions do not transfer intellectual property rights.

2) Operator credentials and attestation: Accept ephemeral credentialing as the default for witnessed sessions. Require an operator session attestation signed by a Redwood authorized officer that includes session start/end, operator identity, actions performed (high level), hashes of artifacts delivered, and a statement that no persistent operator credentials were provisioned to Customer infrastructure.

3) Proof‑of‑access window & delivery: Exhibit to include: (a) maximum scheduling window (e.g., 12 months from execution for standard support; custom terms negotiable), (b) artifacts delivered (session logs, signed attestation, artifact hashes, optional screen share recording where permitted), and (c) retention period for delivered artifacts (suggest 90 days by default).

4) Escrow‑Lite stewarding: Draft a tri‑party clause naming steward responsibilities, chain of custody, and the trigger events that permit stewarded access (e.g., insolvency, failure to meet SLA thresholds, or written request per order form). Keep pricing carveouts explicit in the order form.

5) Indemnity & liability: No change to core indemnity; add a limited carveout that steward or witness activities do not expand Redwood liability beyond the MSA allocation.

Action for Alexandria: if you can confirm the typical retention period and custody transfer steps, we'll put the above into an Exhibit G draft and flag any carveouts for procurement.

--
Lauren Bishop
Deputy General Counsel, Redwood Inference
Attachment: proposed_exhibit_G.docx (docx)

title: Request: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review
preview:
From: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: compliance@pinebrookhealth.com
Date: 2026-06-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Ben —

Thanks for taking this. Pinebrook is kicking off an external SOC 2 readiness review and our auditor has asked for a snapshot of 'admin activity' (console actions, privilege grants/revokes, role changes, API key lifecycle) covering 2025-10-01 through 2026-05-31. They want:

- A single export (or an attestable bundle) with time-ordered admin events
- Chain-of-custody proof (signed manifest, checksums) and timezone normalization notes
- Any redaction options for customer-identifiable fields and how sampling is performed

Primary constraints: we need delivery by 2026-06-25 and a quick handshake on transport (SFTP or pre-authenticated GCS link). Can you confirm what you can provide and what the expected file shapes are?

Lisa Murphy
Director, Compliance
Pinebrook Health


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: compliance@pinebrookhealth.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Hi Lisa —

Thanks — we can produce a time-ordered export and an attested manifest. Quick clarifying notes/questions to speed the handoff:

1) Scope: you listed console actions & API key lifecycle. Do you also want operator session metadata (login IPs, MFA outcomes) and workspace-level policy changes?

2) Redaction: we can redact email addresses and customer identifiers at export time, or provide full export and a redaction map. Which does the auditor prefer?

3) Transport & signing: we typically deliver via SFTP (mutual key) or an encrypted GCS/S3 presigned object, plus a signed CSV manifest that includes file checksums (sha256) and an HMAC over the manifest for attestation. We can also timestamp and notarize the manifest if required.

4) Format: default export is newline-delimited JSON (ndjson) with a small summary CSV manifest. Can provide parquet on request.

I'll loop in Kimberly (security product) to confirm the export window and retention carve-outs; Logan will coordinate transfer ops.

Action: Kimberly, can you confirm redaction defaults and whether we need a sampled subset or full export for Pinebrook?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales (Security Liaison)
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-22T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Lisa / Ben —

We can do a full export for the window requested. A few implementation details so the auditor can sign-off without follow-ups:

- Event set: admin actions include: role.create/role.update/role.delete, policy.attach/detach, api_key.create/revoke/rotate, session.login, session.logout, privilege.grant/privilege.revoke, console.command.exec, and system:config.change. Each record includes event_ts (UTC), actor_id (internal id), actor_display, target_resource, resource_type, change_summary, request_id, and trace_id.

- Timezones: we canonicalize to UTC in the export. We will include original tz offsets where available (e.g., originating IP geolocation) in the event metadata.

- Redaction approach: by default we PII-mask email addresses and IPs in the exported bundle and provide a sealed 'redaction_matrix.xlsx' describing applied transforms. If the auditor requires raw fields, we can produce an auditor-only bundle under a separate NDA/handshake.

- Sampling vs full: for SOC 2 auditors prefer full or deterministic slices. We'll provide the full export by default, but we can also include a deterministic sampling manifest if Pinebrook prefers a smaller subset for initial review.

- Checksums & attestation: we'll produce sha256 checksums per file + a signed manifest (RSA-2048) and an HMAC wrapping of the manifest per Pinebrook's shared secret.

Logan — please prepare SFTP credentials (or presigned GCS) and include sftp_handshake_instructions.txt. We'll attach the export_manifest_template.csv to show the schema.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Product Security Manager
Redwood Inference


From: Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-23T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Team / Lisa —

SFTP is ready. Options:

- Option A: Mutual-key SFTP (preferred). We provide host key fingerprint and you drop an upload user key. Transfer will land in /incoming/pinebrook/2026-06-25/ and we rotate keys after delivery.

- Option B: Encrypted presigned GCS link (24h TTL).

Attached: sftp_handshake_instructions.txt (stub) contains the fingerprint, preferred ciphers, and required ACLs.

Thanks for taking this. Pinebrook is kicking off an external SOC 2 readiness review and our auditor has asked for a snapshot of 'admin activity' (console actions, privilege grants/revokes, role changes, API key lifecycle) covering 2025-10-01 through 2026-05-31. They want:

- A single export (or an attestable bundle) with time-ordered admin events
- Chain-of-custody proof (signed manifest, checksums) and timezone normalization notes
- Any redaction options for customer-identifiable fields and how sampling is performed

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
To: Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: compliance@pinebrookhealth.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Hi Lisa —

Thanks — we can produce a time-ordered export and an attested manifest. Quick clarifying notes/questions to speed the handoff:

1) Scope: you listed console actions & API key lifecycle. Do you also want operator session metadata (login IPs, MFA outcomes) and workspace-level policy changes?

2) Redaction: we can redact email addresses and customer identifiers at export time, or provide full export and a redaction map. Which does the auditor prefer?

3) Transport & signing: we typically deliver via SFTP (mutual key) or an encrypted GCS/S3 presigned object, plus a signed CSV manifest that includes file checksums (sha256) and an HMAC over the manifest for attestation. We can also timestamp and notarize the manifest if required.

4) Format: default export is newline-delimited JSON (ndjson) with a small summary CSV manifest. Can provide parquet on request.

I'll loop in Kimberly (security product) to confirm the export window and retention carve-outs; Logan will coordinate transfer ops.

Action: Kimberly, can you confirm redaction defaults and whether we need a sampled subset or full export for Pinebrook?

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Enterprise Sales (Security Liaison)
Redwood Inference


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Lisa Murphy <lisa.murphy@pinebrookhealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Logan Wright <logan@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-06-22T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: admin activity export & chain-of-custody for SOC 2 review

Lisa / Ben —

We can do a full export for the window requested. A few implementation details so the auditor can sign-off without follow-ups:

- Event set: admin actions include: role.create/role.update/role.delete, policy.attach/detach, api_key.create/revoke/rotate, session.login, session.logout, privilege.grant/privilege.revoke, console.command.exec, and system:config.change. Each record includes event_ts (UTC), actor_id (internal id), actor_display, target_resource, resource_type, change_summary, request_id, and trace_id.

- Timezones: we canonicalize to UTC in the export. We will include original tz offsets where available (e.g., originating IP geolocation) in the event metadata.

- Redaction approach: by default we PII-mask email addresses and IPs in the exported bundle and provide a sealed 'redaction_matrix.xlsx' describing applied transforms. If the auditor requires raw fields, we can produce an auditor-only bundle under a separate NDA/handshake.

- Sampling vs full: for SOC 2 auditors prefer full or deterministic slices. We'll provide the full export by default, but we can also include a deterministic sampling manifest if Pinebrook prefers a smaller subset for initial review.

- Checksums & attestation: we'll produce sha256 checksums per file + a signed manifest (RSA-2048) and an HMAC wrapping of the manifest per Pinebrook's shared secret.

Logan — please prepare SFTP credentials (or presigned GCS) and include sftp_handshake_instructions.txt. We'll attach the export_manifest_template.csv to show the schema.

Kimberly Park
Sr. Product Security Manager
Redwood Inference

title: POC activation: auth/keys, IP allowlist, and VPC peering checklist
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samantha Wu <samantha.wu@luminahealth.com>
Cc: Alex Torres <alex.torres@luminahealth.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: POC activation: auth/keys, IP allowlist, and VPC peering checklist

Hi Samantha —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Attached is a draft Mutual Action Plan (Lumina_MAP_v1.1.pdf) that frames the POC activation steps we discussed. High level asks for the activation sprint:

- Org setup: tenant seed + service account to bind to your test org
- API keys: short-lived test keys (ephemeral) for early QA, long-lived scoped keys for canary runs
- IP allowlist: two public CIDR ranges that we'll hit from Redwood hosted runtime (CSV attached)
- VPC peering: template and target ACLs (see vpc-peering-template.yml)

Can you confirm the following by EOD Tuesday?
1) Point person for network/VPC peering (name + email)
2) Preferred peering window (we typically schedule 1–2 hour window for routing/tunnel validation)
3) Any internal ALLOWLIST or firewall constraints we should be aware of (port, next-hop, NAT rules)

Proposed timeline (we can compress if you’re ready):
- Day 0 (kickoff): finalize MAP and sign-off on IP ranges
- Day 1: org seed + ephemeral test keys issued
- Day 2: VPC peering established & smoke tests
- Day 3–5: workload smoke, metrics baseline, rollback/contingency validation

Attachments: Lumina_MAP_v1.1.pdf, lumina-ip-allowlist.csv, vpc-peering-template.yml

Happy to hop on a 30 min call to walk the checklist with your network/security.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0162
