On Mon, Jun 21, 2026 at 12:10 PM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Naomi / Hannah — looping you both in to coordinate the peering and key provisioning for the LuminaHealth POC.

Thanks Ben — notes below and a compact checklist for Thursday's window. Attaching the vpc-peering-template.yml we use and an ephemeral-key README.

Checklist (action owners):
1) Confirm Lumina CIDRs for allowlist (Alex) — we will whitelist during smoke phase. Recommended: provide /28s or /29s to avoid accidental broad blocks.
2) Ephemeral keys (Naomi) — generating scoped test keys with TTL=8h. Delivery: secure hubshare link, will expire after 48h.
3) VPC peering (Hannah) — we require: peering request, remote VPC ID, remote ASN (if BGP), and expected tentative CIDR ranges. For non-BGP static routes we need next-hop and NAT status.
4) Smoke tests (Samantha+Ben) — endpoints to hit: 
   - GET /health (220ms SLA target)
   - POST /v1/generate (sample request attached)

Technical details / defaults:
- Redwood source IPs (sample): 34.201.12.0/28, 52.40.88.0/28 (please confirm in your CSV)
- Ports: 443 outbound only; our load balancer terminates TLS
- Ephemeral keys: header X-Redwood-Test-Key: <token>

Attachments referenced: vpc-peering-template.yml, lumina-ip-allowlist.csv (please confirm exact CIDR lines), ephemeral-key-README.md

We'll aim to generate the keys and post them to the secure link by 07:00 PST Thursday, which should give you time to stage tests before the peering window. If Alex can confirm CIDRs today, Hannah and I will proceed.

Thanks,
Naomi Feldman
Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
naomi.feldman@redwood.com

title: Staged upgrade vs add-on burst — guidance for Sunrise Health
preview:
From: Liz Carter <liz.carter@sunrisehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Staged upgrade options and feature bundling question

Hi Chloe —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Our engineering team ran a short pilot and we expect steady token volume growth over Q3 (attached forecast). Two quick questions as we plan next steps:

1) We're exploring either a stepwise upgrade to the next committed tier (to get higher sustained throughput) OR keeping current plan and buying short-term burst add-ons for known peak windows. From a pricing and feature perspective, what are the trade-offs?

2) We also use a small set of advanced features (priority routing and per-route observability). Are those bundled with the higher tier or only available as add-ons? If bundled, does upgrading early unlock them immediately or is there an activation lag?

We'd like a concise recommendation — our procurement and engineering leads are on the thread (cc'd). Prefer a call tomorrow afternoon if that helps.

Attachment: sunrise_usage_forecast_q3.xlsx

Thanks,
Liz
Liz Carter
Director, AI Platform
Sunrise Health


From: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
To: Liz Carter <liz.carter@sunrisehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Staged upgrade options and feature bundling question

Hi Liz —

Appreciate the forecast — that's helpful. Short answer up front: both paths are viable; the right one depends on how long you expect the elevated windows to persist and whether you need guaranteed SLAs for those peaks. High-level comparison: 

- Temporary burst add-on (monthly):
  - Pros: low friction, no long-term commit, immediate capacity for peaks.
  - Cons: higher unit cost during bursts, not covered by committed-throughput SLA, limited to best-effort KV caching across bursts.

- Stepwise upgrade to next committed tier: 
  - Pros: lower average unit cost, included advanced features (priority routing, per-route traces) once provisioned, covered by SLA for sustained throughput.
  - Cons: commit term and potential overprovision if peaks are short-lived.

- Hybrid (recommended if peaks are seasonal): commit to a modest uplift now + short-term burst credits for the first 2–3 months while you validate uplift behavior. This gives you feature access and better unit economics while avoiding overcommit.

Activation notes: bundled advanced features are enabled within 24–48 hours after tier change; observability retention windows are adjusted at provisioning time (no code changes on your side).

I'll draft a simple quote and a comparison table and loop Ben for discount guidance (cc'd).

Next steps:
- I can send a short formal quote with hybrid pricing by EOD.
- If that looks good, we can schedule a 30-min sign-off call tomorrow at 2pm PT.

Draft attached: chloe_proposal_draft.pdf (contains a scenario table: burst-only, step-up, hybrid).

Best,
Chloe
Chloe Martin
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
chloe.martin@redwood.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 13:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Staged upgrade options and feature bundling question

Chloe —

Thanks for the context and the draft. For Sunrise (DEAL-9817) I recommend we present the hybrid path with the following commercial terms:

- 6-month uplift to the next tier at a 10% discount to list committed throughput price (applies to the additional committed units only).
- 1 month of burst credits (covering anticipated Q3 peaks) at cost + 5% admin fee.
- One-time onboarding credit equal to 25% of the first month uplift cost to offset migration work.

If Sunrise moves to a 12-month commitment on the uplift, we can increase the discount to 15%. Approve the 10%/6-month approach if you want a cleaner, faster paper trail.

A couple of legal/commercial notes: the uplift will include immediate activation of priority routing and per-route traces; observability retention increases will be reflected in the quote. SLA for the uplifted portion becomes effective at provisioning; burst credits remain best-effort and are not SLA-backed.

Happy to jump on the call for final pricing justification.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Sales Ops, Redwood Inference


From: Liz Carter <liz.carter@sunrisehealth.com>
To: Chloe Martin <chloe.martin@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Alvarez <tom.alvarez@sunrisehealth.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 16:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Staged upgrade options and feature bundling question

Thanks Chloe and Ben — that framing is exactly what we needed.

We're inclined to accept the hybrid approach: modest uplift for sustained coverage + short burst credits while we validate. Two asks before we sign:

1) Please send the updated quote with the 6-month, 10% uplift terms and the onboarding credit called out separately.
2) Confirm how we can revert the uplift if we realize the uplifted capacity is too large after 60 days (what's the proration & rollback process?).

Would like the updated quote today if possible — Tom and I will review and book the 2pm PT call if it looks clean.

Hi Gloria — thanks for the request and thanks Ben for the starter package. 

Sharing notes:
- SOC 2: attaching the latest SOC 2 Type II summary report (redwood_soc2_type2_2025.pdf). The full report is covered by our attestation agreement and we typically deliver it under NDA during contract negotiations.
- KMS / key residency: Redwood uses customer-specific ephemeral keys for inference sessions when customers enable \"ephemeral encryption\"; root KMS keys are hosted in vendor-managed HSMs in the region of the customer's dedicated deployment. We do not retain customer plaintext outside the configured region for dedicated/private deployments. For hosted API customers we use multi-region key management but restrict exports — see the short note below.
- Telemetry: platform health telemetry (CPU, GPU utilization, request latency distributions, anonymized error traces) is collected and stored in internal metrics systems. No customer prompt or response content is forwarded to analytics vendors. We do send aggregated usage metrics to our analytics provider (rollup only, no identifiers).

Attachments provided here: redwood_soc2_type2_2025.pdf (summary), and I also reattached the roster and SIG-lite from Ben for reference. 

If Aurelia needs the full SOC 2 bundle, happy to share under an NDA. If you want, we can lock a short bridge call — 20 minutes — to walk through residency and telemetry controls.

title: Vendor onboarding: PO visibility, portal handback SLA & email ingest fallback
preview:
From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the onboarding call this week. Finance asked me to send the example invoice and confirm the preferred intake for Acme moving forward:

- Do you prefer we push invoices into your portal (preferred) or email billing as a fallback?
- If email, what subject token or filename rules help automate PO linking?
- Any constraints on multi-PO invoices (we often combine 2 POs on one PDF).

I've attached a sample invoice (sample_invoice_acme.xlsx) and our ERP export sample. Happy to pilot whichever you recommend.

Best,
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[sample_invoice_acme.xlsx]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables: 

1) Portal (preferred):
   - Upload CSV or PDF via supplier portal. Portal will automatically match PO numbers to the ledger when the PO is present in the subject/metadata.
   - We expose an "upload confirmation" (handback) within ~1 hour for successful automated matches; full processing/GL mapping completes within 24 hours.

2) Email fallback:
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO<po-number> (e.g., "INV:PO12345"). For multi-PO, use commas: INV:PO12345,PO12346.
   - Filename convention: invoice_<supplierinvoiceid>.pdf or .csv (invoice_INV12345.pdf).
   - We can accept PDFs (preferred) and machine-readable CSVs. Avoid embedded images-only PDFs (OCR is slower).

3) Multi-PO rules:
   - If you include multiple POs on one invoice, include a short mapping CSV in the email body or as a second attachment with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_total.

I'm attaching our lightweight template (redwood_invoice_template.csv) — it's minimal but aligns with our ledger fields. We'll pilot portal next week; if you want, Monica can run a quick preflight on two sample invoices.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Procurement Partnerships | Redwood Inference
[redwood_invoice_template.csv]

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Thanks Aditya — that helps. A couple of clarifying Qs from our side before we spin up the portal pilot: 

- Handback latency: you mention ~1 hour for automated matches — what does the handback payload include (ACK vs full line-item match)?
- If we send a single PDF that contains multiple PO sections, do you need a separate CSV mapping every time? We could also embed a short CSV in the body if the portal is unavailable.

Our AR team uses QuickBooks and prefers PDFs, but we can generate a CSV export for the portal if that speeds automated matching. Also, confirm whether we should put our internal supplier invoice id in both the filename and the PDF header to improve de-duping.

Cheers,
Sara

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 11:05 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables...

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Adding a quick operational playbook from ops side to make this pilot low-friction: 

- Portal-first: supplier portal ingestion (CSV preferred) will provide a 1-hour ACK when invoice file lands and a processing status update (MATCHED / PENDING / ERROR). ACK includes: supplier_invoice_id, received_timestamp, matched_po_numbers (if any), and an intake_id we use for troubleshooting.

- Email fallback (only when portal inaccessible):
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai
   - Required: Subject token exactly INV:PO<po#> or INV:PO<#>,<#> for multiple (no spaces around commas).
   - Attachments: include a PDF (final invoice) and optionally a small mapping CSV (filename: invoice_mapping_<supplier_invoice_id>.csv) with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_net_amount.
   - Filename preference: invoice_<supplier_invoice_id>.pdf. We rely on filename + subject token to reduce manual triage.

- SLA & exceptions: ACK within 24 hours for email submissions (we aim for same-day), portal ACK within 1 hour for successful automated ingestion. Complex multi-PO PDFs may require manual intake — add the mapping CSV to avoid that.

I'll have Ops enable a short validation rule that rejects image-only PDFs and flags them for manual processing. Monica — can you run the two-sample preflight with Acme and confirm expected ACK payload?

—Jordan
Senior Vendor Operations | Redwood Inference

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Perfect — appreciate the clarity. We'll: 

1) Pilot portal on Wed 24 Jun with two invoices (one single-PO PDF, one multi-PO PDF + mapping CSV).
2) Also send the same files to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO67890,PO67891 as a controlled fallback so you can validate email flow.
3) We'll include supplier_invoice_id in both filename and PDF header.

Attachments to expect from us for the pilot: invoice_INV12345.pdf, invoice_INV12346.pdf, invoice_mapping_INV12346.csv

Icc'ing our AR lead in case you need additional ERP exports: billing@acmecorp.com

Thanks — talk soon.
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics

text:
From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for the onboarding call this week. Finance asked me to send the example invoice and confirm the preferred intake for Acme moving forward:

- Do you prefer we push invoices into your portal (preferred) or email billing as a fallback?
- If email, what subject token or filename rules help automate PO linking?
- Any constraints on multi-PO invoices (we often combine 2 POs on one PDF).

I've attached a sample invoice (sample_invoice_acme.xlsx) and our ERP export sample. Happy to pilot whichever you recommend.

Best,
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[sample_invoice_acme.xlsx]

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables: 

1) Portal (preferred):
   - Upload CSV or PDF via supplier portal. Portal will automatically match PO numbers to the ledger when the PO is present in the subject/metadata.
   - We expose an "upload confirmation" (handback) within ~1 hour for successful automated matches; full processing/GL mapping completes within 24 hours.

2) Email fallback:
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO<po-number> (e.g., "INV:PO12345"). For multi-PO, use commas: INV:PO12345,PO12346.
   - Filename convention: invoice_<supplierinvoiceid>.pdf or .csv (invoice_INV12345.pdf).
   - We can accept PDFs (preferred) and machine-readable CSVs. Avoid embedded images-only PDFs (OCR is slower).

3) Multi-PO rules:
   - If you include multiple POs on one invoice, include a short mapping CSV in the email body or as a second attachment with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_total.

I'm attaching our lightweight template (redwood_invoice_template.csv) — it's minimal but aligns with our ledger fields. We'll pilot portal next week; if you want, Monica can run a quick preflight on two sample invoices.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Procurement Partnerships | Redwood Inference
[redwood_invoice_template.csv]

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 08:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Thanks Aditya — that helps. A couple of clarifying Qs from our side before we spin up the portal pilot: 

- Handback latency: you mention ~1 hour for automated matches — what does the handback payload include (ACK vs full line-item match)?
- If we send a single PDF that contains multiple PO sections, do you need a separate CSV mapping every time? We could also embed a short CSV in the body if the portal is unavailable.

Our AR team uses QuickBooks and prefers PDFs, but we can generate a CSV export for the portal if that speeds automated matching. Also, confirm whether we should put our internal supplier invoice id in both the filename and the PDF header to improve de-duping.

Cheers,
Sara

On Thu, Jun 18, 2026 at 11:05 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Hi Sara — thanks for sending this over and for the clear sample. Short answers up front: portal-first, email-only as a defined fallback. A few specifics so your team can map deliverables...

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica_patel@redwood.ai>, Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 11:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Adding a quick operational playbook from ops side to make this pilot low-friction: 

- Portal-first: supplier portal ingestion (CSV preferred) will provide a 1-hour ACK when invoice file lands and a processing status update (MATCHED / PENDING / ERROR). ACK includes: supplier_invoice_id, received_timestamp, matched_po_numbers (if any), and an intake_id we use for troubleshooting.

- Email fallback (only when portal inaccessible):
   - Send to invoices@redwood.ai
   - Required: Subject token exactly INV:PO<po#> or INV:PO<#>,<#> for multiple (no spaces around commas).
   - Attachments: include a PDF (final invoice) and optionally a small mapping CSV (filename: invoice_mapping_<supplier_invoice_id>.csv) with columns: supplier_invoice_id,po_number,line_net_amount.
   - Filename preference: invoice_<supplier_invoice_id>.pdf. We rely on filename + subject token to reduce manual triage.

- SLA & exceptions: ACK within 24 hours for email submissions (we aim for same-day), portal ACK within 1 hour for successful automated ingestion. Complex multi-PO PDFs may require manual intake — add the mapping CSV to avoid that.

I'll have Ops enable a short validation rule that rejects image-only PDFs and flags them for manual processing. Monica — can you run the two-sample preflight with Acme and confirm expected ACK payload?

—Jordan
Senior Vendor Operations | Redwood Inference

From: Sara Thomas <sara.thomas@acmecorp.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.ai>
Cc: Billing Team <billing@acmecorp.com>
Date: Mon, 22 Jun 2026 14:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Onboarding + initial invoice sample

Perfect — appreciate the clarity. We'll: 

1) Pilot portal on Wed 24 Jun with two invoices (one single-PO PDF, one multi-PO PDF + mapping CSV).
2) Also send the same files to invoices@redwood.ai with Subject: INV:PO67890,PO67891 as a controlled fallback so you can validate email flow.
3) We'll include supplier_invoice_id in both filename and PDF header.

Attachments to expect from us for the pilot: invoice_INV12345.pdf, invoice_INV12346.pdf, invoice_mapping_INV12346.csv

Icc'ing our AR lead in case you need additional ERP exports: billing@acmecorp.com

Thanks — talk soon.
Sara Thomas
Billing Operations | Acme Analytics
[invoice_INV12345.pdf]

title: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation
preview:
From: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 14:05:00 -0400
Subject: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Hi Ava,

As we get into renewal planning for our account, Connor and I want to schedule the QBR + roadmap demo. Our execs have very tight calendars next week and would prefer a compact, focused format rather than a full-day workshop.

High-level ask: can Redwood run a short, split-format QBR — one exec-facing 30-minute review (business impact + renewal ask) and two 45-minute lane-specific deep dives (product roadmap and ops/compliance)? We think that will get the right stakeholders in for the right parts without losing momentum.

Preferred windows (ET):
- Tue 6/22, 11:00–13:30 ET (we can do an 11:00–11:30 exec slot followed by the dives)
- Wed 6/23, 14:00–16:30 ET

Must-haves: a short attendee matrix upfront so we can confirm who attends which lane, and a SOC 2 / data handling blurb in the ops/compliance lane (Connor will loop Legal if needed).

Attaching an attendee matrix draft for your host planning.

Thanks,
Amelia
Amelia Cross
Head of Platform Partnerships | Bluehaven Health
[attachment: attendee-matrix.csv]


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Cross <amelia@bluehaven.com>
Cc: Stephanie Nguyen <stephanie.nguyen@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Amelia — thanks for the clear ask and the attendee matrix. We can absolutely run a compact, lane-based QBR. Proposed structure below (I built it so we can run it as a tight 2.5-hour block with a host rotation and a recorded exec summary):

Agenda proposal (total 2.5 hours):
- 11:00–11:30 ET: Exec review (Ava + exec owner) — renewal context, top metrics, ask
- 11:35–12:20 ET: Product roadmap lane (Stephanie) — feature highlights, roadmap, prioritized asks (45m)
- 12:25–13:10 ET: Ops & compliance lane (Rishi) — deployment model, SOC2/data handling, support SLA (45m)
- 13:10–13:25 ET: Quick next-step and action owner wrap (10–15m)

Host rotation: Ava will open the exec review and run the wrap; Stephanie will lead product lane; Rishi will lead compliance lane and has the SOC2 summary. We’ll record the exec review and provide the recording + one-pager afterwards.

We can do Tue 6/22 starting at 11:00 ET as you suggested — I’ll hold the slot tentatively. Linked below are the draft agenda and a DocuSign NDA we typically send before demos with sensitive access.

Attachments: draft-agenda.pdf (attached), attendee-matrix.csv (from you)
Links: https://calendar.google.com/event?eid=abc123def456, https://www.docusign.net/link-to-NDA-xyz

If that works, I’ll send calendar invites with explicit lane invites (exec only; product + ops attendee lists) and a short pre-read.

Best,
Ava Chen
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | +1 415 555 0190

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Connor Patel <connor@bluehaven.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 09:08:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Renewal QBR — compact lanes and host rotation

Thanks, Ava — that structure looks great. Two small adjustments from our side:

1) CEO constraint: our CEO can only join 11:45–12:15 ET on Tue. Can we shift the exec review to start at 11:30ET and run 11:30–12:00ET instead? That would still give you time for the lane dives afterward if we compress slightly.

2) Ops/legal ask: Connor’s legal lead may join for a 15–20 minute SOC2 discussion; could Rishi have a 10-slide compliance one-pager available?

We confirm the attendee matrix and will finalize the list by EOD Monday.

Forwarding your schedule suggestion below for context.

Rishi — can you confirm you have the SOC2 one-pager and a 10-slide backup for Legal? I attached a draft compliance one-pager you can reuse (compliance-onepager.pdf). Also: please capture the exact question set we should expect from Bluehaven Legal — Connor, if you can share any specifics, that helps us prepare.

Action items:

title: Ridgeway Labs pilot: success metrics, access needs, and kickoff timing
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@ridgeway.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Ridgeway Labs pilot: success metrics, access needs, and kickoff timing

Alex — thanks for the call this morning. Quick written recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth.

Summary of pain points you shared:
- High p99 latency during peak trading windows (spikes >1s) impacting analyst experience.
- Unpredictable inference costs across bursty query patterns.
- Data residency/encryption constraints for certain EU clients.

Pilot goals (what success looks like):
1) Reduce inference p99 for the query->response flow to <200ms for 95% of traffic.
2) Demonstrate a ~30% reduction in cost per 1M tokens for the summarization workload vs your current setup.
3) Validate secure VPC connectivity and audit logging for the demo dataset.

Proposed POC scope (4 weeks):
- Use cases: real-time query understanding (low-latency path) + nightly batch summarization (throughput/cost path).
- Deliverables: latency/cost baseline, indexed prompt set + eval suite, deployment options (Dedicated vs Private), rollout playbook.
- Success metrics: p50/p95/p99, cost per 1M tokens, model output quality on our prompt eval (precision/recall/ROUGE for summarization).

Access & items we need from Ridgeway:
- Sample prompt set and ~3k representative queries (S3 link or secure upload).
- A service account/email for IAM provisioning and your networking contact for VPC peering.
- Any compliance docs (SOC2 contact) if we scope Private deployment.

Next steps / owners:
- Camila: finalize kickoff agenda and calendar invite (target: Wed 2026-06-24).
- Aditya: propose VPC/architecture options and expected latency delta (due: Mon 2026-06-22).
- Ben: draft preliminary sizing and ballpark cost delta for Dedicated (due: Tue 2026-06-23).
- Monica (CS): assemble prompt eval harness and example metrics template.

Attachment: poc_success_metrics.xlsx (success metric template & baseline sheet)
Recording: https://drive.google.com/recording/ridgeway-discovery-20260621

Thanks — happy to adjust anything missing.

—Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com


From: Alex Turner <alex.turner@ridgeway.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Soma Ghosh <soma@clearbridge.ai>
Date: 2026-06-21T12:10:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Ridgeway Labs pilot: success metrics, access needs, and kickoff timing

Camila — great write-up, thanks. A few clarifications:
- Can we scope the POC to two canonical flows: (A) live query routing (low-latency) and (B) nightly summarization? That's aligned with our internal priorities.
- For compliance: we need TLS in transit + customer-managed KMS for keys if we go Private. Is that supported out of the box?
- Cost: can Ben provide a 90/10 confidence band (optimistic / conservative) for Dedicated pricing? We won't sign anything without a committed range.

Proposed kickoff: Wed 2026-06-24 at 09:00 PT works for our team. Please send calendar invite and dial-in.

Also looping in Soma from our infra vendor for VPC questions.

Thanks,
Alex

Alex Turner
Head of ML Platforms
Ridgeway Labs
alex.turner@ridgeway.com


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-21T13:05:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Re: Ridgeway Labs pilot: success metrics, access needs, and kickoff timing

Forwarding Alex's notes — action items below.

Action items (internal):
- Aditya: confirm Private deployment supports customer-managed KMS + TLS; sketch VPC peering checklist and required ports. Deliverable: short architecture note and estimated latency impact for low-latency path. Due: 2026-06-22 EOD.
- Ben: provide a 90/10 cost band & initial sizing for Dedicated for the two flows (live query + nightly summarization). Include token assumptions. Due: 2026-06-23 noon PT.
- Monica: prepare prompt eval harness and populate the metric template (p50/p95/p99, ROUGE, latency histogram). Also draft the kickoff agenda. Due: 2026-06-23 EOD.

I'll send the 6/24 kickoff invite once we have the agenda. Copying Alex + Soma for transparency.

Onward,
—Camila

text:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Alex Turner <alex.turner@ridgeway.com>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>

Subject: Ridgeway Labs pilot: success metrics, access needs, and kickoff timing

Alex — thanks for the call this morning. Quick written recap and proposed next steps so we have a single source of truth.

Summary of pain points you shared:
- High p99 latency during peak trading windows (spikes >1s) impacting analyst experience.
- Unpredictable inference costs across bursty query patterns.
- Data residency/encryption constraints for certain EU clients.

Pilot goals (what success looks like):
1) Reduce inference p99 for the query->response flow to <200ms for 95% of traffic.
2) Demonstrate a ~30% reduction in cost per 1M tokens for the summarization workload vs your current setup.
3) Validate secure VPC connectivity and audit logging for the demo dataset.

Proposed POC scope (4 weeks):
- Use cases: real-time query understanding (low-latency path) + nightly batch summarization (throughput/cost path).
- Deliverables: latency/cost baseline, indexed prompt set + eval suite, deployment options (Dedicated vs Private), rollout playbook.
- Success metrics: p50/p95/p99, cost per 1M tokens, model output quality on our prompt eval (precision/recall/ROUGE for summarization).

Access & items we need from Ridgeway:
- Sample prompt set and ~3k representative queries (S3 link or secure upload).
- A service account/email for IAM provisioning and your networking contact for VPC peering.
- Any compliance docs (SOC2 contact) if we scope Private deployment.

Next steps / owners:
- Camila: finalize kickoff agenda and calendar invite (target: Wed 2026-06-24).
- Aditya: propose VPC/architecture options and expected latency delta (due: Mon 2026-06-22).
- Ben: draft preliminary sizing and ballpark cost delta for Dedicated (due: Tue 2026-06-23).
- Monica (CS): assemble prompt eval harness and example metrics template.

Attachment: poc_success_metrics.xlsx (success metric template & baseline sheet)
Recording: https://drive.google.com/recording/ridgeway-discovery-20260621

Thanks — happy to adjust anything missing.

—Camila Reyes

Redwood Inference

Quick notes:
- We should avoid US late-afternoon which is late-night in SG. Best overlap windows typically are: early US morning / late EU afternoon / evening SG, or late US evening / early EU morning / morning SG — both have tradeoffs for on-call.
- Proposal: provide two safe multi-day options and one compromise that sacrifices one region's convenience (we can rotate for future sessions).

Option A (US-anchored morning):
- UTC 13:00–14:15 (Boston 09:00–10:15 EDT, Berlin 15:00–16:15 CEST, Singapore 21:00–22:15 SGT)
Pros: Boston morning, Berlin afternoon; con: Singapore evening.

Option B (EU-anchored afternoon):
- UTC 15:30–16:45 (Boston 11:30–12:45 EDT, Berlin 17:30–18:45 CEST, Singapore 23:30–00:45 SGT)
Pros: Berlin comfortable; con: Singapore late-night shortfall for some.

Option C (APAC-early compromise):
- UTC 04:30–05:45 (Boston 00:30–01:45 EDT, Berlin 06:30–07:45 CEST, Singapore 12:30–13:45 SGT)
Pros: Singapore midday; con: Boston overnight, Berlin early morning.

I recommend we lead with Option A (most friendly to US and EU, tolerable evening for SG if they can do it once). If they prefer rotating convenience we can propose a follow-up with Option C as the standard for future hands-on labs.

Attached: timezone-overlap-matrix.csv (rows: UTC, Boston, Berlin, Singapore) and a two-slide agenda PDF for the deep-dive.

title: Account ownership clarification: BrightWorks / Acme Corp
preview:
From: Dana Morris <dana.morris@brightworks.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Account ownership question - Acme Corp

Hi Camila —

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We’ve started joint outreach at Acme and heard from their procurement team that they were engaged by NimbleTech earlier this quarter. NimbleTech lists BrightWorks as a partner for the East region, and from our side Acme is treated as a West-focused footprint. There’s overlapping contacts in the account and the AE on NimbleTech’s side (cc’d below in their outreach) is pushing for joint references.

Can we get clarity on account ownership and a simple map of who owns which geo/segments for Acme? NimbleTech is pushing hard for a joint case study, and BrightWorks wants to avoid stepping on their sales process.

Quick context: BrightWorks is proposing to lead Commercial and Services for North America East; NimbleTech expects to lead existing DevOps/Infra relationships which overlap with our Platform conversations.

Appreciate a short proposal we can share with NimbleTech on how to split coverage and escalation.

Best,
Dana

Dana Morris
Head of Partnerships, BrightWorks
Dana.morris@brightworks.com
+1 415-555-0192


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Sergio Alvarez <sergio@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 10:14:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Account ownership question - Acme Corp

Team — forwarding BrightWorks' ask. Key points for our internal mapping:

- Account: Acme Corp (global) — existing procurement thread with NimbleTech (they shared an SOW redline three weeks ago).
- BrightWorks wants East commercial lead; NimbleTech claims existing DevOps relationships in East & Central.
- We have product evaluation leads in West and APAC (managed by our CS/solutions contacts).

Proposal draft (what to confirm before we send externally):
1) Ownership split by responsibility, not strictly geo: BrightWorks leads Commercial & Services in NAM-East; NimbleTech leads Infra/DevOps integrations across NAM; Redwood owns platform-level product discussions and reference approvals globally.
2) Define single escalation contact per function (sales, security, services) — reduces dual-ownership friction.
3) Formalize a short SLA for lead-handshake: 48-hour response for inbound enterprise intents; recorded in a shared spreadsheet and mirrored in Salesforce (link in related_links).

Questions for Marcus/Laura: are there any legal/comp concerns if we publicly assign BrightWorks as commercial lead in East while NimbleTech continues integration work? Sergio — can you surface any pre-existing co-sell commitments with NimbleTech on Acme?

I’ll draft a one-page ownership proposal to send to BrightWorks + NimbleTech that keeps our position neutral but protective of Redwood references.

Thanks,
Camila

Camila Reyes
Senior Partner Manager, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com

- Redwood ownership: Platform & reference governance — we approve product messaging and any customer reference usage. Redwood to be looped for legal/SLAs.

Escalation matrix (short):
- Sales escalation: BrightWorks AE -> NimbleTech AE -> Redwood Partnerships (Camila)
- Security/compliance: NimbleTech Security -> BrightWorks Security -> Redwood Security (Sergio)
- Services delivery: BrightWorks Services -> NimbleTech Services -> Redwood CS (Laura)

Operational asks:
1) BrightWorks to confirm commercial lead assignment for NAM-East by COB 6/21.
2) NimbleTech to confirm technical lead and provide the SOW version they referenced.
3) Redwood will create the shared account map spreadsheet and update the SFDC record with the ownership notes (link included).

Thanks,

title: Feature cartridge proposal and tier swap considerations
preview:
From: Emma Li <emma@healthgrid.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-15T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Packaging options for pilot + feature requirements

Hi Vivek,

Thanks for the demo yesterday — the latency and dedicated capacity numbers look promising. We are trying to decide how to structure a 6-month pilot and then scale. A couple of constraints from my side:

- We need the ability to toggle a small set of expensive features (OCR enrichment, PII detection) for a subset of traffic.
- Budget for the pilot is constrained; procurement is asking for predictable monthly commitments rather than pure metering.

Can you send options that let us:
1) start on a smaller reserved tier and toggle the two premium features for 10% of calls, and
2) an upgrade path if we double traffic mid-pilot?

Also, what flexibility do you have on pilot discounts and crediting unused reserved capacity?

Thanks — happy to hop on a 30-min call if that helps.

Best,
Emma
Emma Li
Director, Platform Partnerships
HealthGrid



From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Emma Li <emma@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-16T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Packaging options for pilot + feature requirements

Hi Emma — great to meet you yesterday. Appreciate the clear constraints. High-level options below; I attached a short matrix with numbers.

Option A — Reserved small + feature cartridges (recommended for low risk)
- Reserved tier: Small (2 reserved vGPU slots) monthly commit, predictable cost.
- Feature cartridges: OCR & PII as add-on cartridges that can be enabled per-route; cartridge licensing billed at a flat monthly cartridge fee (applies if enabled for any traffic) plus a variable per-call surcharge. For partial rollouts we can gate by route or token percent.
- Upgrade path: Can move to Medium tier (4 slots) mid-month with prorated billing; cartridges transfer across tiers.

Option B — Metered + soft-reserve (flex)
- Lower commit, pay-per-token for baseline, with an option to cap spend. Add-ons billed per-call. Useful if you want low upfront.

For the pilot discount: we can offer a 35% credit on reserved fees (applied as monthly credits) for a 6-month pilot if you commit to a roadmap that includes a formal review at month 3 and a scaling plan. Unused reserved capacity can be 50% creditable toward the next month during the pilot (to reduce waste).

I'll defer to Laura on the operational specifics and to our commercial team on final discounting cadence — copying her here. If you like, I can convert Option A into an unambiguous quote and DocuSign link.

Best,
Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Sales, Platform
Redwood Inference



From: Emma Li <emma@healthgrid.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-17T08:43:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Packaging options for pilot + feature requirements

Vivek — this is helpful, thank you. Two quick clarifications before we take to procurement:

1) Cartridge billing: does the flat monthly cartridge fee apply even if the feature is enabled for only one route? Procurement is nervous about paying for a feature that will be used sparingly.
2) Pilot discount: 35% reserved fee credit is a good start. Can you get to 50% applied as upfront credits? Procurement often favors larger upfront credits to keep OPEX low.

If you can send a sample quote with Option A + a line showing the 50% pilot credit vs 35% I can push it through faster.

Thanks — Emma



From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-17T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Packaging options for pilot + feature requirements — pricing guidance request

Folks — forwarding HealthGrid thread. They asked if we can: 1) make cartridge fee proportional to enablement (less punitive for low usage), and 2) move pilot reserved credit from 35% up to 50% as upfront credits.

Quick ask: what's our tolerance on the cartridge model for pilots (reduce flat fee / bill a microflat + per-call) and can we approve a 50% pilot credit for a 6-month, committed pilot that has a clear month-3 review? Related: this is a likely upsell (doubling after pilot).

Notes: HealthGrid wants predictable monthly fees, low OPEX during pilot, and the ability to toggle the two expensive features for ~10% traffic.

Forwarded message attached below.

Thanks,
Vivek



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-18T09:30:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Packaging options for pilot + feature requirements — pricing guidance request

Vivek — short answer: yes on a pilot concession if it has clear success metrics and a formal review. My recommended structure:

- Pilot reserved credit: 45% upfront credit (midpoint between 35/50) tied to a signed MOU that documents the month-3 review and a go/no-go scaling decision. If legal needs 50 to close, escalate; otherwise 45 is standard.
- Cartridge model for pilots: reduce flat monthly cartridge fee by 60% and introduce a small per-call surcharge so customers aren’t punished when usage is low. After pilot, revert to standard cartridge pricing unless they commit to a higher tier.
- Add clause: unused reserved capacity crediting capped at 50% to next month during pilot only.

If Karthik/Laura sign off on the operational feasibility, I’ll approve commercial.

-Ben


From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek@redwood.com>
To: Emma Li <emma@healthgrid.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-06-20T16:45:00-07:00

- Reserved tier: Small (2 vGPU slots) monthly commit.
- Pilot credit: 45% of reserved fees applied as upfront monthly credits across the 6-month pilot. (Ben approved 45% as the standard concession; if procurement insists on 50% please flag and I will escalate.)
- Feature cartridges: OCR & PII modeled as cartridges with the following pilot pricing: 60% reduction to the normal monthly cartridge fee + a $0.002 per-call surcharge for calls routed through the cartridge. Cartridge fee will apply if enabled for any route; we can support per-route enablement to limit exposure.
- Unused reserved capacity: up to 50% creditable toward the following month during the pilot only.
- Upgrade path: mid-month upgrade to Medium (4 slots) available; proration applies and cartridges transfer.

Attachments: redwood_feature_cartridge_pricing.xlsx (quote), healthgrid_feature_map.pdf (routing & routes-to-enable). DocuSign link: https://docusign.com/XYZ-HealthGrid-quote

Next steps:
- If that aligns, say yes and I’ll push a formal MOU and DocuSign for signature.
- If you want Procurement to see an example with 50% instead of 45%, tell me and I’ll route to leadership for an override.

Happy to jump on a 20-min call to walk procurement through the cartridge mechanics.

Best,

Redwood Inference

title: Dev tools renewal: GitHub / Jira / JetBrains — sourcing & consolidation review
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-10T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Upcoming renewals — quick sync request

Team —

Heads-up: our agreements for GitHub Enterprise, Atlassian cloud products (Jira/Confluence) and JetBrains IDE seats all roll in July/Aug. I pulled the current quotes and added a simple savings model (attached). Before we engage renewals formally I want a short internal review to decide whether to:

- consolidate licensing across orgs (reduce seat sprawl)
- renegotiate enterprise discounts and term lengths
- consider third-party procurement help for consolidated negotiation

Ask: can each of you review the attached model and highlight any hard constraints (team migrations, security requirements, seat owners) by end of week? I propose a 30m sync next Tue to align on strategy.

Attachments: Consolidation_Savings_Model_v2.xlsx

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Director, Platform Engineering
Redwood Inference



From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-11T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming renewals — quick sync request

Ben —

Thanks for kicking this off. A few notes after skimming the model:

- GitHub: we have a handful of enterprise-only repos owned by security-sensitive projects; those can't move off our current org without approvals. I'll compile a list.
- JetBrains: Engineering has several floating licenses via credit card payments for contractors. Consolidation would require procurement support to migrate billing.
- Atlassian: several project spaces in Confluence are owned by Product and Legal; product docs retention is sticky.

I can pull seat counts by org and a list of outstanding contractor seat payments by EOD Thu. Sync next Tue works — please add Samir from GitHub and Priya from Atlassian to the thread once we decide to engage.

- Marissa
Senior Engineering Manager



From: Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-05-12T08:27:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Upcoming renewals — quick sync request

Ben/Marissa —

Counters from infra side:
- GitHub Enterprise: our SSO/OAuth integration is currently in a standard org. Migrating repos or changing org boundaries in a renewal window is high-risk. If we pursue consolidation, we must push migration work out 2+ sprints and include rollback docs.
- JetBrains: most SREs use toolbox licenses; we can convert to an enterprise subscription but need a 60d runway for license assignment and to clear contractor invoices.

I recommend: negotiate pricing and term length first (get quotes), then commit to migration only after approvals and a cutover plan. Happy to join vendor calls.

—Dev
Staff Reliability Engineer



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>, Samir Ahmed <samir.ahmed@github.com>, Priya Mehta <priya.mehta@atlassian.com>, Elena Petrova <sales@jetbrains.com>
Date: 2026-05-18T09:40:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Dev tools renewal — request for initial commercials

Team + vendors —

Per internal review we want to collect firm commercial offers from each vendor. High-level ask: share a 12/24-month pricing option, volume discounts for consolidated seats, academic/nonprofit discounts where applicable, and any migration/onboarding credits. We are evaluating a single-vendor savings credit if we consolidate JetBrains procurement and Atlassian/GitHub under a single reseller.

Marissa/Dev will own the technical constraint list and seat inventory; Lauren will steward procurement terms. Please send initial quotes by 5/28 so we can run numbers and decide on a negotiation lead.

Ben

--- Forwarded attachments: GitHub_quote_2026_Q3.pdf; Jira_pricing_sheet_May2026.xlsx; JetBrains_offer_summary.pdf



From: Samir Ahmed <samir.ahmed@github.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Elena Petrova <sales@jetbrains.com>, Priya Mehta <priya.mehta@atlassian.com>
Date: 2026-05-21T14:15:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Dev tools renewal — request for initial commercials

Ben/All —

Thanks for the clear ask. Attaching our standard 12/24-month quotes plus a consolidated seat discount model based on your current footprint. Highlights:
- 15% list discount on enterprise seats for 24-month term; additional 5% if GitHub Actions usage credits exceed threshold.
- Migration assistance: $15k credit for onboarding if you consolidate billing under one purchasing entity.
- Security add-on pricing unchanged.

We can schedule a 30m call next week to walk the numbers. If you want to include a reseller for a single-invoice model we can support that.

Attachments: GitHub_quote_2026_Q3.pdf

Best,
Samir Ahmed
Enterprise Sales, GitHub



From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Dev Patel <dev.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Samir Ahmed <samir.ahmed@github.com>, Priya Mehta <priya.mehta@atlassian.com>, Elena Petrova <sales@jetbrains.com>, Acme Procurement Consulting <procure@acme-consult.com>
Date: 2026-06-20T16:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Dev tools renewal — sourcing recommendation and next steps

All —

Quick update / recommendation after reviewing vendor offers and our constraints:

Summary:
- Best commercial packaging: pursue 24-month terms with GitHub and Atlassian; JetBrains should remain on 12-month for flexibility but move to consolidated billing.
- Estimated blended savings (consolidate + negotiation): ~22% over current spend (see attached model).
- Migration constraints flagged by Dev & Marissa mean we cannot do a hard repo/org migration before Oct; so we will: negotiate now, lock commercial terms (with deferred migration credits), and schedule technical cutovers for Q4.

Action items:
1) Ben/Marissa: finalize seat inventory and security-only repo list by 6/25.
2) Lauren: engage procurement to draft master purchase orders and start reseller conversations (Acme) — target term sheet by 7/2.
3) Marissa/Dev: schedule technical readiness review for Sept (include SSO/OAuth rollback plan).
4) Finance: approve payment terms (30/60/NET) — CC Chris in Finance (I will loop him in).

Attachments referenced: Consolidation_Savings_Model_v2.xlsx, Jira_pricing_sheet_May2026.xlsx, GitHub_quote_2026_Q3.pdf, JetBrains_offer_summary.pdf

If no objections I will proceed to draft the negotiation plan and set vendor calls for the week of 6/28.

Lauren Bishop
Procurement Lead
Redwood Inference

To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwoodinference.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean.gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Fri, 19 Jun 2026 09:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Private deployment pricing: platform fee vs metered — Acme RFP

Folks —

Thanks for the context. Below is a pragmatic commercial structure that balances Acme's capex/opex asks and our unit economics. Numbers are rough — I can refine if you want a playbook table.

1) Commitment bands (annual committed spend):
   - Band A: $250k/year — list discounts: 10% off hosted list; platform fee $40k/year (or $3.3k/mo amortized).
   - Band B: $500k/year — list discounts: 18%; platform fee $30k/year (or $2.5k/mo amortized).
   - Band C: $750k+/year — list discounts: 25%; platform fee $20k/year (or $1.7k/mo amortized).

2) Pilot treatment:
   - Offer a 60-day pilot with up to $25k usage credit that does not count against the annual commit if they sign within 45 days of pilot close. This helps remove friction and keeps pilot from eating into committed capacity.

3) Migration / onboarding credit:
   - Conditional credit: up to 25% of professional services fees (capped at $120k) applied as an invoice credit over first 3 monthly invoices if customer signs Band C (>= $750k).
   - Alternative: $50k one-time onboarding credit if customer prepays annual fee.

4) Support tiers (recommended mapping):
   - Standard (included): business hours support, 48-hr SLA for severity 1
   - Premium: 24/7 monitoring + 4-hr response for sev1, dedicated CS engineer (adds $6k/mo)
   - Enterprise: 24/7 on-call with guaranteed on-site/remote runbook triage within 2 hours, uptime SLA 99.95% (adds $13k/mo + optional oncall retainer)

5) Billing / booking notes (Sean):
   - We can amortize the platform fee for invoicing purposes; Finance should be okay as long as we have a signed SOW capturing the amortization schedule. If they insist on a single upfront platform fee, expect different revenue recognition.

6) Holdbacks / redlines to avoid:
   - No unlimited credits or open-ended PS estimates. Credits must be conditional on commit or prepay.
   - Keep on-call response SLAs tied to the support tier purchase.

If that sounds good, I'll draft a short one-pager and a recommended clause for the PS credit.

-Vivek
VP, Solutions & Partnerships | Redwood Inference

title: Approval needed: NewCo 3yr price-stability & renewal protection
preview:
From: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@newco.ai
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Hi Aditya / Laura,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the team was impressed with the latency and routing controls. We're preparing a recommended vendor list for our platform consolidation and would like to propose a 3-year agreement with two commercial asks:

- A firm price lock for the first 24 months, with a capped uplift of +5% for years 3 (not to exceed +8% over the entire term).
- A renewal-protection clause: in the event NewCo is acquired or changes cloud region, we need the option to renew at the same unit economics or a pre-agreed formula.

Attached is the RFP summary and our standard procurement template. Can you confirm if Redwood can accept these two asks and whether legal will accept the renewal language? We have a June 25 procurement board meeting that we are targeting.

Best,
Caroline
Caroline Hsu
Head of Procurement, NewCo
---
Attachment: NewCo_RFP_summary.pdf (pdf, 312 KB)

----
From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 10:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Caroline —

Appreciate the clear asks and the timeline. Short answer: we can support a 24-month fixed-price window and a capped uplift; the renewal-protection clause is policy-dependent and will need a one-off approval for an exception to our standard renewal terms.

Next steps I propose:
1) Marissa (deal desk) to draft a commercial terms sheet with the proposed cap language (+5% year 3, cumulative not to exceed +8%).
2) Laura (GC liaison) to review the renewal-protection wording and flag any redlines.
3) I'll loop Sean from Sales Ops for capacity commit guidance and to confirm dedicated pool availability for the committed throughput.

We can target internal approvals this week so you can bring to your board June 25. If approved I will escalate to the exec desk for an approval memo.

Thanks,
Aditya
Aditya Rao
Head of Strategic Accounts, Redwood Inference

----
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Cc: Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 09:26:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Aditya / Laura / Caroline —

Draft commercial term sheet attached. Key commercial points I included:
- Term: 36 months with price lock on months 1–24.
- Uplift: Year 3 uplift capped at +5% relative to year 2. Aggregate uplift across term not to exceed +8%.
- Renewal-protection: We grant a one-time right to match unit economics for a 12-month renewal if change-of-control or region migration occurs, provided contract is renewed within 90 days of the triggering event and committed throughput is at least 70% of prior year.
- Early-termination: standard pro-rata termination fee applies for early cancellation during committed term.

Notes / ask from legal: the renewal-protection clause contains a subjective \"change-of-control\" definition and a throughput threshold — Laura, please review and advise if we should tighten.

Attachment: proposed_terms_redline_v2.docx

Marissa
Sr. Deal Desk Manager, Redwood Inference

----
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Caroline Hsu <caroline@newco.ai>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 14:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: 3-year price lock + renewal protection

Team —

I've flagged three legal points in the attached redline:
1) Define \"change-of-control\" narrowly to include 50%+ transfer of voting interest or acquisition of substantially all assets.
2) Clarify what constitutes \"region migration\" (majority of traffic migrating regions vs. single-project migration).
3) Add a clause requiring NewCo to provide audited usage reports within 60 days of a renewal request to validate throughput eligibility.

If we agree to those amendments, I recommend an executive pricing exception for the renewal-protection right — it creates a business-risk vector if we are forced to match economics without being able to validate usage. I will prepare a short memo for the exec desk describing legal exposure and recommended guardrails.

Laura Bennett
Deputy General Counsel, Redwood Inference

Attachment: legal_redline_terms.docx

----

- Risk: Laura to include wording that the renewal match is conditional on audit-based verification and limited to a single 12-month renewal period.

Action items for exec review:

3) Authorize Sales Ops to hold dedicated capacity conditional on signed SOW and forecast by July 2.

I will assemble the 1-page memo and route to the pricing execs (VP Revenue Ops and CFO) for sign-off. Caroline — once we have exec approval we'll push a final contract redline for your procurement board.

Draft commercial term sheet attached. Key commercial points I included:
- Term: 36 months with price lock on months 1–24.
- Uplift: Year 3 uplift capped at +5% relative to year 2. Aggregate uplift across term not to exceed +8%.
- Renewal-protection: We grant a one-time right to match unit economics for a 12-month renewal if change-of-control or region migration occurs, provided contract is renewed within 90 days of the triggering event and committed throughput is at least 70% of prior year.
- Early-termination: standard pro-rata termination fee applies for early cancellation during committed term.

Notes / ask from legal: the renewal-protection clause contains a subjective \"change-of-control\" definition and a throughput threshold — Laura, please review and advise if we should tighten.

Attachment: proposed_terms_redline_v2.docx

Marissa
Sr. Deal Desk Manager, Redwood Inference

2) Clarify what constitutes \"region migration\" (majority of traffic migrating regions vs. single-project migration).
3) Add a clause requiring NewCo to provide audited usage reports within 60 days of a renewal request to validate throughput eligibility.

title: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@acmehealth.com>, Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:03:00 -0700
Subject: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices

Hi Elena / procurement team,

Thanks again for the productive call last week. As discussed, Acme will receive a one-time service credit for the initial onboarding ramp; we want to make sure that credit shows up on your invoices in a way that matches your internal cost-center mapping and local VAT rules.

Quick proposal attached (service-credit-allocation-v1.xlsx):
- We can issue a consolidated monthly invoice that lists line items by cost center with entity-level allocation tags.
- The credit will appear as a negative line item that references the original invoice number (so your AP system can auto-apply).
- For multi-entity customers (US + EU), we suggest splitting taxable lines between Redwood, Inc. (US) and Redwood GmbH (EU) based on the seat/cost-center mapping you provide. The spreadsheet contains a sample mapping and export-ready CSV format.

Questions for you:
1) Do you prefer a single consolidated invoice with allocation rows, or two separate entity invoices with a reconciliation report?
2) Can you share the cost-center codes that should be used for the allocated lines?
3) Please confirm VAT registration numbers / billing addresses for EU entity processing (we have Redwood GmbH VAT info ready to include).

Next steps: if you confirm the mapping we will:
- Prepare an amended SOW that notes the credit application method (DocuSign link to follow).
- Send a sample consolidated invoice PDF for your AP team to validate.

Attaching the allocation template for your team to fill in. Happy to hop on a 30-minute call if that helps.

Best,
Camila Reyes
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
Redwood Inference

Attachments: service-credit-allocation-v1.xlsx


From: Elena Morales <elena@acmehealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>, Jared Li <jared.li@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices

Camila / Ben,

Thanks for the template — useful. A couple of clarifications from our side:

- VAT / tax: For the EU portion we need Redwood GmbH to include its VAT number and show the reverse-charge wording if applicable. Will the negative credit line also include VAT impact or should we expect the credit to be net of VAT?
- Consolidation: We prefer a single consolidated invoice if the allocation rows are machine-readable (CSV-friendly) so our ERP can split to cost centers automatically.
- Refund vs credit: The credit should apply to future invoices (negative line item). Please confirm that credits are not refunded.

Also: Attached is our initial cost-center mapping (separate file sent internally to procurement). Can Redwood include the customer-side cost-center as a separate column on the invoice line? That would speed reconciliation.

Can you also include the legal names and tax IDs of both Redwood entities? Procurement will need that for our AP vendor record.

Happy to schedule a brief call if needed.

Thanks,
Elena Morales
Sr. Manager, Finance Operations
Acme Health
elena@acmehealth.com


From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 14:07:00 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Elena Morales <elena@acmehealth.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Acme Health Procurement <procurement@acmehealth.com>, Jared Li <jared.li@acmehealth.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Jun 2026 11:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices

Team — forwarding Acme's questions. They want:
- legal names and VAT/TIN for both entities,
- confirmation that credit is a negative line applied to future invoices (no cash refund),
- consolidated invoice with CSV-ready allocation rows and customer cost-center column.

Ben / Karthik / Marissa, can you confirm we can produce that format and whether there are tax nuances for the EU split (reverse charge vs VAT on credit lines)? I attached the draft allocation spreadsheet.

Thanks,
Camila

Attachments: service-credit-allocation-v1.xlsx, amended-sow-acme-draft.pdf


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena@acmehealth.com>, Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 17:12:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Service credit allocation schema for split invoices

Hi Elena and team,

Thank you — great questions. Confirmations and proposed approach below:

1) Legal / tax ids:
- Redwood, Inc. (US) — Redwood Inference, Inc. / EIN: 12-3456789
- Redwood GmbH (EU) — Redwood Inference GmbH / VAT ID: DE 123456789
(we will include full registered addresses on the invoice)

2) Credit behaviour:
- Credits will be issued as negative line items on subsequent invoices and will not be refunded in cash (per standard T&C). We will include the originating invoice number in the line description for traceability.

3) VAT treatment for the credit and split lines:
- For US-domiciled seats billed by Redwood, Inc. we will show no VAT.
- For EU-domiciled seats billed by Redwood GmbH we will apply VAT handling consistent with reverse-charge when Acme is VAT-registered (please supply your VAT number).
- Credits: the negative line will mirror the VAT treatment of the original charge. If the original line was reverse-charged, the credit will be reverse-charged as well (i.e., customer self-accounting on VAT).

4) Invoice format / machine-readability:
- We can produce a consolidated PDF and a companion CSV that includes: invoice_line_id, entity_code (US/EU), customer_cost_center, description, qty, unit_price, tax_code, tax_amount, line_net, line_gross. The attached spreadsheet includes the CSV template.

5) Next steps & SOW amendment:
- We'll prepare an amended SOW that states the credit method and the split allocation approach. I'll circulate a DocuSign envelope for signature once Camila confirms.
- If helpful, I can join a 30-minute AP/finance alignment call next week to validate the CSV import with Acme's ERP team.

Please let us know Acme's preferred billing cadence (monthly / net-30 / net-45) for application of the credit.

Best regards,
Karthik Iyer
Director, Finance Operations
Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.com

Attachments referenced: amended-sow-acme-draft.pdf (draft), service-credit-allocation-v1.xlsx (CSV template)

title: Insurance-backed penalty proposal & stepdown thresholds for SLA breaches
preview:
From: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Ava —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Heliox is framing our procurement ask around not just credits but an enforceable upside for us if SLA misses are repeated. Rather than escrow or large immediate termination rights, procurement proposed an insurance-backed construct that pays capped penalties after sustained breaches. Attached is a simple model for the mechanics (heliox-request-model.xlsx). Key asks: 

- Insurance-backed fund: insurer issues a performance bond that pays penalties up to 6% of monthly committed fees per month in the worst tier.
- Stepdown thresholds: penalties escalate after 3 consecutive weekly SLO misses (tier 1), and again after 6 consecutive misses (tier 2) with higher payout.
- Termination: right to terminate only after 3 months of tier-2 level breaches, not on a single incident.

We prefer this instead of a straight termination clause or credit-only approach. Can Redwood confirm feasibility and any regulatory or accounting concerns? Also looping in procurement for visibility.

Tom
Tom Baxter
Director, Procurement
Heliox Therapeutics

Attachments: heliox-request-model.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Tom —

Appreciate the clear framing and the model — that helps a lot. Short answer: the concept is something we can work with, but there are a few practical considerations on our side (insurance procurement path, capital exposure, and how penalties interact with Dedication vs Hosted plans).

High-level thoughts: 
- Insurance bond is an interesting alternative to escrow; it reduces the cash on our balance sheet but creates administrative overhead. We need to validate whether our insurers will underwrite this with SLA-language tied to external monitoring metrics.
- The stepdown thresholds are reasonable, but we push to define SLO measurement sources (Redwood Console vs Heliox synthetic checks) and a reproducible reconciliation window.
- Termination only after sustained Tier-2 makes sense; we would ask for a cure period and a remediation playbook before termination triggers.

I've attached an initial draft of the clauses we would expect to see (redwood-draft-terms.docx). Ben and Marissa are on copy to weigh in on commercial and legal risk. Can we set a quick 30-min sync next week to walk through the model?

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx (draft terms)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — commercial input

Ava / Marissa —

Quick notes from commercial perspective after reading Tom's model and Ava's draft: 

- Fee-at-risk sizing: Heliox's model assumes up to 6% per month in worst-case payouts. We should counter with a cap tied to committed spend (e.g., max 12% of monthly committed fee per quarter) to protect runway.
- Pricing impact: If an insurer is involved, there will be an effective premium or monitoring fee — we should ask Heliox to accept that premiums cannot be underwritten by Redwood for long-term deals; alternatively a pass-through or shared-cost model.
- Monitoring: Recommend using Redwood Console as source-of-truth for SLA failures but allow Heliox to submit supplemental synthetic evidence with defined window (48 hrs).

I'm fine with the tiered stepdown approach but want to add an automatic review meeting when Tier-1 is hit twice in a rolling 90-day window — gives us time to debug before Tier-2.

Ben
Ben Carter
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — legal comments

Team —

Legal thumbs-up in principle with the insurance-backed mechanism; a few required guardrails: 

- Clear metric definitions: \"SLO misses\" must be unambiguous (p99 latency > X ms over a 1-hour window OR availability < 99.5% per week). Avoid vague language that insurers won't accept.
- Indemnity carve-outs: Redwood needs to ensure penalties don't cascade with other indemnities or create double recovery scenarios.
- Audit rights: Redwood will require insurer confirmation language and a process for validating paid claims. We should insist on an explicit insurer contact and SLA for payout timing.
- Termination: recommend keeping a remediation plan and a final 30-day cure period after Tier-2 before termination is available — this aligns with our operational playbook.

I'll draft markups into the redwood-draft-terms.docx and share. Also recommend we propose a shared-cost model for the insurance premium (split 70/30 Heliox/Redwood) or ask Heliox to obtain the bond directly to avoid our balance-sheet exposure.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds — Redwood counter

Tom —

Thanks for your patience while we reviewed your proposal. Attached is a concise counter that preserves your intent but addresses Redwood's operational and legal constraints (see summary below and attached risk-stepdown-chart.png). We'd like to move to a short call to nail down the monitoring and insurer mechanics. Proposed summary: 

- Cap: Penalties funded via an insurer bond capped at 10% of a rolling three-month committed fee (reduces volatility).
- Tiers: Tier-1 (3 consecutive weekly SLO misses) — remediation meeting + 2x credits; Tier-2 (6 consecutive) — insurer payout up to capped amount and formal remediation plan.
- Cure & termination: 30-day cure after Tier-2 before termination available; termination only if remediation fails and Tier-2 breaches continue.
- Premiums: Proposed shared-cost model 60% Heliox / 40% Redwood for initial year; revisit on renewal. If Heliox prefers to source insurer directly, Redwood will accept insurer onboarding provided insurer language is pre-approved.

Attachments provided: redwood-draft-terms.docx (updated with our markups), risk-stepdown-chart.png (visual of triggers). Also sharing a DocuSign stub for the commercial letter if you want to fast-track acceptance: https://docusign.com/heli-penalty-proposal/DS-9F3

Can we take 30 minutes next Tue (23rd) or Wed (24th) to finalize? If yes, please pick a slot and we'll get calendar invites out.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx, risk-stepdown-chart.png

- Audit rights: Redwood will require insurer confirmation language and a process for validating paid claims. We should insist on an explicit insurer contact and SLA for payout timing.
- Termination: recommend keeping a remediation plan and a final 30-day cure period after Tier-2 before termination is available — this aligns with our operational playbook.

I'll draft markups into the redwood-draft-terms.docx and share. Also recommend we propose a shared-cost model for the insurance premium (split 70/30 Heliox/Redwood) or ask Heliox to obtain the bond directly to avoid our balance-sheet exposure.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds — Redwood counter

Tom —

Thanks for your patience while we reviewed your proposal. Attached is a concise counter that preserves your intent but addresses Redwood's operational and legal constraints (see summary below and attached risk-stepdown-chart.png). We'd like to move to a short call to nail down the monitoring and insurer mechanics. Proposed summary: 

- Cap: Penalties funded via an insurer bond capped at 10% of a rolling three-month committed fee (reduces volatility).
- Tiers: Tier-1 (3 consecutive weekly SLO misses) — remediation meeting + 2x credits; Tier-2 (6 consecutive) — insurer payout up to capped amount and formal remediation plan.
- Cure & termination: 30-day cure after Tier-2 before termination available; termination only if remediation fails and Tier-2 breaches continue.
- Premiums: Proposed shared-cost model 60% Heliox / 40% Redwood for initial year; revisit on renewal. If Heliox prefers to source insurer directly, Redwood will accept insurer onboarding provided insurer language is pre-approved.

Attachments provided: redwood-draft-terms.docx (updated with our markups), risk-stepdown-chart.png (visual of triggers). Also sharing a DocuSign stub for the commercial letter if you want to fast-track acceptance: https://docusign.com/heli-penalty-proposal/DS-9F3

Can we take 30 minutes next Tue (23rd) or Wed (24th) to finalize? If yes, please pick a slot and we'll get calendar invites out.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx, risk-stepdown-chart.png
